articles.headline: Israel strikes Iranian nuclear facilities following threats of escalation
articles.summary: Israel attacked Iran's nuclear sites on Friday, including a heavy-water plant in Arak and a yellowcake production facility in Yazd. The Israeli military claimed responsibility for the strikes, stating they targeted missile production and nuclear infrastructure. Iranian authorities confirmed the attacks caused no casualties or contamination but threatened retaliation for the strikes.

articles.headline: Jaishankar raises Global South energy concerns at G7 meeting
articles.summary: External Affairs Minister S Jaishankar flagged concerns regarding energy, food, and fuel security for the Global South during a G7 foreign ministers' meeting in France. He held bilateral talks with counterparts from the US, Japan, and France to discuss the West Asia conflict and the security of the Strait of Hormuz. India emphasized the need for resilient trade corridors like IMEC.

articles.headline: India backs Bangladesh quest for justice over 1971 Pakistani atrocities
articles.summary: The Ministry of External Affairs officially supported Bangladesh's desire for justice regarding the 1971 genocide committed by the Pakistani military. Spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal referred to the 'terrible atrocities' and 'targeted killing of millions' during the liberation war. The statement follows Bangladesh's 'Genocide Day' observations and calls for Pakistan to apologize.

articles.headline: Iran Guards turn away ships; Strait of Hormuz closed to enemies
articles.summary: The IRGC turned back three container ships trying to transit the Strait of Hormuz, declaring the route closed to vessels linked to 'enemies.' Among the vessels were two ships belonging to Chinese firm COSCO. Iran's foreign minister stated the strait remains open only to 'friendly nations' like India, China, and Russia, while others are being blocked on a case-by-case basis.

articles.headline: Iran military warns hotels housing US troops are targets
articles.summary: Iran's armed forces spokesperson warned that hotels in the Middle East housing US soldiers will be treated as military targets. The warning comes after reports that US troops relocated to hotels and offices in the UAE and Bahrain following strikes on their original bases. Iran urged local hotels to deny bookings to US personnel to avoid being caught in the conflict.

articles.headline: UAE to participate in maritime taskforce to reopen Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: The United Arab Emirates has informed Western allies it will join a multinational taskforce to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The UAE is also collaborating with Bahrain on a UN Security Council resolution to defend shipping from Iranian attacks. The vital waterway is essential for UAE's oil exports, which have faced repeated Iranian strikes during the conflict.

articles.headline: China launches investigations into US trade ahead of Xi-Trump summit
articles.summary: China has initiated probes into US trade practices as a retaliatory measure against Trump administration policies. The Ministry of Commerce announced the investigations ahead of a mid-May summit between President Xi Jinping and President Trump. The move seeks to gain leverage in trade disputes involving record surpluses and US arms sales to Taiwan.

articles.headline: Iran-Linked Hackers Breach FBI Director Kash Patel’s Personal Email
articles.summary: Iran-linked "Handala Hack Team" claimed a breach of FBI Director Kash Patel’s personal email, publishing photos and documents. A Justice Department official confirmed the breach and the authenticity of published material. Handala is believed to be an Iranian government cyberintelligence unit.

articles.headline: Trump delays Iran strikes again, claims "talks" are going well
articles.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump announced a pause on any potential strikes on Iran's power plants until April 6, stating that "talks" with the Islamic Republic are going "very well." Iran, however, denies holding direct negotiations. The IRGC claimed attacks on Israeli targets in Ashdod and U.S. bases in UAE and Kuwait.

articles.headline: Jaishankar calls for stronger trade corridors, supply chains amid West Asia war
articles.summary: External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar discussed India and the Global South’s concerns about food, fuel, and fertilizer shortages with G-7 counterparts. He underscored the importance of resilient trade corridors and supply chains, stating that the uncertainties from the West Asia conflict only make a stronger case for corridors like IMEC (India Middle East Europe Economic Corridor).

articles.headline: Iran FM accuses U.S. of 'calculated' assault on school
articles.summary: Iran’s Foreign Minister on Friday branded a deadly strike on an Iranian school, occurring on the first day of the West Asia war, as a "calculated, phased assault." Abbas Araghchi stated that "more than 175 students and teachers were slaughtered in cold blood" in the February 28 strike on an Iranian school in Minab.

articles.headline: India sends invites for BRICS meetings amid West Asia conflict
articles.summary: Hoping to forge an elusive consensus over the war in West Asia, New Delhi, as current Chair of BRICS, has sent out invitations for the BRICS Foreign Ministers’ Meeting in May and the 18th BRICS Summit in September. Diplomats have acknowledged difficulties in forging a joint statement acceptable to all members, especially Iran and UAE.

articles.headline: IDF chief warns of potential collapse amid manpower shortage
articles.summary: Israel Defence Forces (IDF) chief Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir reportedly warned a security cabinet meeting that the military "is going to collapse in itself" due to a growing manpower shortage. He highlighted the urgent need for a conscription law, a reserve duty law, and a law to extend mandatory service, amid ongoing conflicts in Iran, Lebanon, and the West Bank.

articles.headline: G-7 allies press U.S. for clarity on Iran war plans
articles.summary: G-7 allies were on Friday pressing U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio for clarity on American plans for Iran, almost one month into the war in West Asia. Concern is intensifying over the extent of cooperation between Russia and the Islamic Republic. In a sign of distance, no joint communique is expected at the end of the meeting.

articles.headline: Myanmar junta signals leadership change ahead of parade
articles.summary: Myanmar’s junta signalled changes in the military’s leadership ahead of the country’s annual show of force on Friday, potentially clearing the way for defence chief Min Aung Hlaing to become President. This move comes after he ousted the government of Aung San Suu Kyi in 2021, leading to civil war.

articles.headline: Trump gives Iran April 6 deadline to reopen Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump has issued a 10-day ultimatum to Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face the destruction of its energy plants. This follows Iran's rejection of US proposals to end the ongoing conflict. Tensions have escalated as Israel reportedly hit targets in Iran, and oil prices have surged due to fears of navigation blocks.

articles.headline: External Affairs Minister Jaishankar emphasizes freedom of navigation at G7
articles.summary: During the G7 meeting in France, India's EAM S. Jaishankar underscored the vital importance of freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz for global energy and food security. He met with US Secretary of State Marco Rubio and other counterparts to discuss resilient supply chains and the security situation in West Asia.

articles.headline: Government hikes commercial LPG supply cap to 70 percent
articles.summary: The petroleum ministry raised the cap on commercial LPG supplies from 50% to 70% of pre-crisis levels. The move provides priority access to labour-intensive industries like steel, automobiles, and textiles. Petroleum secretary Neeraj Mittal informed states that additional allocations aim to support industrial production despite global supply disruptions linked to the Iran conflict.

articles.headline: Finance Minister Sitharaman confirms 4.3 percent fiscal deficit target
articles.summary: Union Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman told the Rajya Sabha that the government will meet its 4.3% fiscal deficit target for 2026-27. Despite a significant loss in fuel excise revenue, the government expects to balance finances through efficient expenditure and higher non-tax revenue. The Parliament approved the Finance Bill 2026, completing the budgetary exercise for the next fiscal.

articles.headline: PLFS 2025 report shows labour market recovery to pre-pandemic levels
articles.summary: The Periodic Labour Force Survey for 2025 shows India's headline unemployment rate remained low at 3.1%. The Labour Force Participation Rate rose to 44.9%, indicating a full recovery from COVID-19 shocks. Growth was highest among salaried workers, reaching 23.6%, while agricultural employment retreated to 43%, nearly matching pre-pandemic baseline figures.

articles.headline: Centre Cuts Excise Duty on Fuel, Imposes Export Levy to Ensure Supply
articles.summary: The government cut excise duty on petrol by ₹10/litre and diesel by ₹10/litre, effective Feb 26, 2026. This aims to shield refiners from losses due to rising global oil prices. Simultaneously, export duties of ₹21.5/litre on diesel and ₹29.5/litre on ATF were imposed to ensure domestic supply. Pump prices remain unchanged for consumers.

articles.headline: Hawala Market Sees Discount on Dollar Amid War and Travel Halt
articles.summary: The dollar-rupee exchange rate in India's informal hawala market has slipped into a rare discount of 1-2% below the official rate. This unusual shift is attributed to stalled flights, reduced smuggled gold from Dubai, and muted trade and travel to and from the Gulf, due to the West Asia conflict.

articles.headline: Finance Minister Assures Fiscal Vigilance Amid Oil Spike
articles.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman stated the government will step up efforts to mobilise additional non-tax revenues and remain vigilant in managing the fiscal deficit. She reiterated the government's strategy to shield consumers from rising crude oil prices, citing the recent fuel duty cuts as a measure to prevent domestic inflation.

articles.headline: Centre Cuts Borrowing Target for FY27, Plans `8.2 Lcr in H1
articles.summary: The Centre will borrow ₹8.2 lakh crore, 51% of its now-reduced ₹16.09 lakh crore target for 2026-27, from the market in the first half of the next fiscal. The scaled-down and prudent borrowing plan aims to calm the bond market amid West Asian war headwinds, with ₹15,000 crore via green bonds.

articles.headline: Parliamentary Panel Flags Low Placements via National Career Service Portal
articles.summary: A parliamentary committee has criticised the labour ministry for low placements through the National Career Service (NCS) portal, with only 35.23 lakh candidates placed against 1.94 crore vacancies. It urged immediate amendments to the portal to track actual placement data effectively and advised the secretary to report changes promptly.

articles.headline: Make Aadhaar-Based Wage Transfer Optional Under MGNREGS: Panel
articles.summary: A parliamentary panel has recommended making the Aadhaar-Based Payment System (ABPS) optional for wage transfers under MGNREGS, suggesting simpler alternatives. While acknowledging ABPS's role in curbing leakages, the panel flagged residual risks of exclusion for genuine beneficiaries, especially elderly workers or those in remote areas with low biometric authentication success.

articles.headline: Advanced Economies Drive Surge in India Remittances
articles.summary: Remittance inflows to India are increasingly driven by higher-skilled migration to advanced economies like the US, UK, Canada, and Australia. India's diaspora sends home $138 billion annually, nearly doubling over a decade and financing almost half of the country's merchandise trade deficit, reducing reliance on Gulf nations.

articles.headline: Fuel Duty Cut, State Debt Issuance Give Bond Market Jitters
articles.summary: Yields on India's 10-year benchmark government bond jumped seven basis points to 6.94% on Friday. This rise is attributed to the Centre's fuel excise duty cut clouding the fiscal outlook amid increasing oil prices, and states bringing in fresh debt supply, leading to waning investor demand for bonds.

articles.headline: RBI Mandates Legal Entity Identifier for Market Participants
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has instructed all market participants, resident and non-resident, to ensure they have a valid Legal Entity Identifier (LEI) code. Entities without an LEI code will not be eligible to undertake transactions in financial markets regulated by the RBI, effective April 10.

articles.headline: Empty LPG Cylinders Fuel Record Piped Natural Gas Demand
articles.summary: India is seeing record additions of piped natural gas (PNG) connections, doubling to 2.5 lakh monthly since the Iran war, due to tight LPG cylinder supplies. Daily additions now range from 9,000-11,000, up from 4,000-5,000, as consumers switch to PNG for uninterrupted supply, freeing up cylinders for the domestic market.

articles.headline: Iran War Tremors Dent Indian Housing Sales in Q1 2026
articles.summary: Housing sales in India's top seven cities dipped 7% to 1,01,675 units in Q1 2026 compared to Q4 2025, reaching ₹1.51 lakh crore. The decline is attributed to war-induced uncertainty, surging oil and construction prices, and prospective Middle Eastern homebuyers deferring investments. However, sales rose 7% annually.

articles.headline: Rupee hits record low of 94.81 as West Asia war rages
articles.summary: The Indian rupee closed at an all-time low of 94.8125 against the US dollar due to surging energy prices and foreign fund outflows. Equities also plummeted, with the Sensex falling 2.25% and Nifty down 2.09%. Bond yields hardened to 6.94% as investors grew concerned over the fiscal implications of the government's recent excise duty cuts.

articles.headline: India faces pressure on WTO investment pact as Turkey withdraws opposition
articles.summary: India's resistance to the China-backed Investment Facilitation for Development (IFD) pact has intensified after Turkey formally withdrew its opposition at the WTO MC14. India remains the sole major holdout, arguing that the plurilateral agreement lacks a legal basis under the Marrakesh Agreement and cluttering the WTO agenda with non-mandated issues.

articles.headline: Exports to West Asia halt as regional conflict enters fourth week
articles.summary: Indian goods exports to West Asia have virtually ceased due to the effective blockage of the Strait of Hormuz. Re-routing shipments via the Cape of Good Hope has increased container costs by up to $2,000. Exporters are facing severe liquidity crunches due to blocked payments. West Asia accounts for approximately 15% of India's total exports.

articles.headline: Supply squeeze and high costs threaten kharif season farmer income
articles.summary: The US-Israel-Iran war is disrupting fertilizer supply chains and hitting domestic urea production due to natural gas shortages. While the government claims a urea reserve of 53.08 lakh tonnes, smallholders face rising input and transportation costs. Officials warn that even a 10-15% hike in non-subsidized fertilizer prices could significantly strain rural incomes.

articles.headline: Centre assures adequate fuel supply and rules out retail price hikes
articles.summary: The government has ruled out retail petrol and diesel price increases despite oil marketing companies (OMCs) facing under-recoveries of ₹26 per litre on petrol and ₹81.9 on diesel. To maintain stability, the Centre approved an additional 20% allocation of commercial LPG for industries. OMCs are currently absorbing combined daily losses of ₹2,400 crore.

articles.headline: Refiners mandated to continue domestic supply of petrol and diesel exports
articles.summary: The government clarified that a 2022 mandate requiring refiners to sell 50% of petrol exports and 30% of diesel exports in the domestic market remains in force. The decision is aimed at ensuring domestic fuel stability amid surging international prices. The Finance Ministry also reimposed a windfall gain tax on aviation turbine fuel and diesel exports.

articles.headline: Piyush Goyal discusses bilateral trade issues with Chinese counterpart
articles.summary: Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal met with Chinese Minister Wang Wentao at the WTO MC14 in Cameroon. The two leaders exchanged views on the MC-14 agenda and discussed bilateral trade matters. The meeting comes as India’s trade deficit with China has crossed the $100 billion mark during the current fiscal year.

articles.headline: States’ borrowing costs rise to over 8% amid global oil shock
articles.summary: Indian states' borrowing costs rose by 0.15-0.3% to cross the 8% threshold at a recent auction. The spike follows global energy price hikes linked to the West Asia conflict. Data from the RBI shows that yields on 25-year bonds climbed by 0.30%, while 13 states successfully borrowed nearly ₹40,000 crore.

articles.headline: Govt to borrow ₹8.2 lakh crore in first half of FY27
articles.summary: The Finance Ministry and RBI have decided to borrow ₹8.20 lakh crore through dated securities during the April-September period. This represents 51% of the total annual gross borrowing of ₹16.09 lakh crore. The plan include ₹15,000 crore of sovereign green bonds. The fiscal deficit for FY27 is projected at 4.3% of the GDP.

articles.headline: West Asia accounts for one-fifth of India’s agricultural exports
articles.summary: Minister Jitin Prasada informed the Rajya Sabha that countries in West Asia contribute nearly 20.5% of India's total agricultural exports, worth $10.68 billion in FY25. The government is monitoring shipping routes and war-risk surcharges affecting the trade of cereals, basmati rice, and meat products to the Gulf Cooperation Council nations.

articles.headline: Kashmir project aims to produce homegrown tulips and bulbs
articles.summary: Scientists at the Mountain Crop Research Station in Kashmir are cultivating indigenous tulip varieties to reduce India's reliance on ₹400 crore worth of annual bulb imports. Supported by Nabard, the project aims to supply bulbs nationwide within three years, cutting costs and stabilizing supply for tourism hubs like the Indira Gandhi Memorial Tulip Garden.

articles.headline: GEMA urges government to raise ethanol blending beyond 20%
articles.summary: The Grain Ethanol Manufacturers Association (GEMA) has called on the Centre to decisively push for ethanol blending levels beyond E20 to enhance domestic energy resilience. Expanding blending targets would create additional demand for agricultural feedstocks like maize and grain, helping maintain fuel price stability for consumers while reducing import dependence.

articles.headline: RBI outlines Vision 2028 roadmap for India's digital payments
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has released 'Payments Vision 2028', which includes initiatives like a switch on/off facility for all digital payment modes and the introduction of electronic cheques. The roadmap focuses on fraud prevention, user empowerment, and cross-border payment efficiency. It also proposes a shared responsibility framework between banks for unauthorized transactions.

articles.headline: Centre relaxes norms to allow BHEL procurements from China
articles.summary: In a significant reset of economic ties, the Centre has allowed BHEL to procure 21 types of critical equipment from countries sharing a land border with India, specifically China. The move eases restrictions on Chinese investments in select sectors to help address capital squeezes after six years of friction between the two nations.

articles.headline: RBI mandates new identifiers for market transparency and OTC transactions
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India has mandated the use of Legal Entity Identifiers (LEI) and Unique Transaction Identifiers (UTI) for financial market participants. The 20-character LEI code will be required for parties in transactions to promote transparency. UTI will be assigned specifically to OTC derivative transactions as part of adopting global standards.

articles.headline: India, US Discuss Next Steps for Bilateral Trade Agreement
articles.summary: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal and USTR Jamieson Greer held discussions on proposed bilateral trade agreement negotiations and WTO MC14 agenda on sidelines of 14th Ministerial Conference in Cameroon. This marks their first in-person meeting since US revoked Trump-era tariffs. US imposed a 10% surcharge on all countries from Feb 24 for 150 days.

articles.headline: Government Discusses E25 Ethanol Blend Shift with Automakers
articles.summary: The Centre initiated talks with automakers on moving to E25 petrol blend, beyond mandatory E20. Auto industry expresses concerns over regulatory, technical, and consumer implications like in-service conformity norms and liability. The shift comes amid West Asia war and high crude prices, with E20 becoming mandatory next month.

articles.headline: Rupee Hits Record Low as West Asia Tensions Spur Market Decline
articles.summary: The rupee slumped to a fresh intraday low of 94.85 against the dollar, becoming Asia's worst-performing currency, dropping over 4% since the West Asia crisis. Government bond yields surged to a near two-year high. Indian equities declined for the fifth consecutive week, with Sensex down 2.3% and Nifty down 2.09%, losing ₹8.92 trillion in investor wealth. Brent crude topped $103/barrel.

articles.headline: Active Foreign Companies in India Decline to 62% in FY26
articles.summary: The share of active foreign companies in India has dropped to 62% (3,293 firms) as of January 2026, from over 70% in FY19, despite an increase in registered foreign companies to 5,311. This indicates many reduced operations or shifted to direct subsidiaries. Net FDI for FY26 is $1.6 billion, a 24% decline from FY25, due to higher capital outflows.

articles.headline: Parliament Approves Finance Bill 2026, Completing Budgetary Exercise
articles.summary: Parliament passed the Finance Bill 2026, with the Rajya Sabha returning it to the Lok Sabha. The FY27 Union Budget projects total expenditure of ₹53.47 trillion (up 7.7%), capital expenditure of ₹12.2 trillion, gross tax revenue of ₹44.04 trillion, and gross borrowing of ₹17.2 trillion. Fiscal deficit is projected at 4.3% of GDP.

articles.headline: RBI Injects ₹65,322 Crore Liquidity via 6-Day VRR Auction
articles.summary: The RBI injected ₹65,322 crore of transient liquidity into the banking system through a six-day variable rate repo (VRR) auction at a cut-off rate of 5.26%. This amount was lower than the notified ₹75,000 crore, despite a sharp drop in surplus liquidity due to advance tax and GST outflows. Liquidity in the system is currently ~₹48,698 crore surplus.

articles.headline: Japan Commits $1.73 Billion Loans for Four Indian Projects
articles.summary: Japan has pledged an Official Development Assistance loan of 275.86 billion yen ($1.73 billion) to India for four projects. The funds will be allocated across key sectors including urban transport, health, and agriculture, reflecting continued bilateral cooperation.

articles.headline: Banking Credit Growth Rises 13.8% in March 15 Fortnight
articles.summary: Banking credit grew 13.8% year-on-year in the fortnight ended March 15, down from 14.5% in the previous fortnight. Deposits saw 10.8% Y-o-Y growth. On an absolute basis, credit increased 0.1% (₹18,672 crore), while deposits contracted 0.7% (₹1.78 trillion), possibly due to advanced tax payments.

articles.headline: Government to Borrow ₹8.2 Trillion in H1FY27
articles.summary: The central government plans to issue ₹8.2 trillion worth of government securities (Gsec) in H1FY27, lower than market expectations. Gross market borrowings for FY27 are ₹17.2 trillion. The borrowing calendar leans towards shorter-tenor bonds, potentially softening bond yields. RBI set Ways and Means Advances limit at ₹2.5 trillion for H1FY27.

articles.headline: RBI OMOs Help Banks Cut Q4 Treasury Losses Amid Surging Yields
articles.summary: The RBI's ₹3.5 trillion bond purchases via open market operations (OMOs) and switch auctions in Q4FY26 are expected to mitigate treasury losses for banks, caused by a 35 basis point surge in 10-year Gsec yields since January, exacerbated by the West Asia crisis. State-owned and large private banks may still face some mark-to-market losses.

articles.headline: Nepal's New PM Faces High Unemployment, Remittance Dependence, Emigration
articles.summary: Nepal's new PM Balendra Shah takes office amidst significant economic challenges. Remittances constituted 26.2% of GDP in 2024, with 76.8% of households receiving them. Nearly 8% of the population emigrated in 2021. Youth unemployment reached 22.7% in FY23, much higher than the national rate of 12.6%.

articles.headline: Finance Minister Dismisses Lockdown Rumours Amid West Asia Crisis
articles.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman dispelled rumors of a lockdown, stating they are spread to create fear. She highlighted that while other countries like Pakistan and Bangladesh face significant price hikes and energy crises, India has kept petrol and diesel prices unchanged, maintaining stability.

articles.headline: Government Increases Commercial LPG Allocation to States by 20%
articles.summary: The central government raised commercial LPG allocation for states by 20%, bringing the total quota to 70% of pre-war demand. This additional supply will prioritize labor-intensive industries like steel, automobiles, and textiles to meet industrial requirements, supporting essential sectors.

articles.headline: China Probes US Trade Practices in Response to Trump Tariffs
articles.summary: China initiated two investigations into US trade practices, signaling pushback against President Trump’s tariffs ahead of his May visit. The probes target US policies restricting Chinese goods and limiting advanced tech exports to China, and barriers to Chinese green energy exports. This is the latest in a long-running trade war.

articles.headline: China's Industrial Profits Surge 15.2% in Early 2026
articles.summary: China’s industrial enterprises reported a 15.2% year-on-year profit increase in the first two months of 2026, the fastest start since 2018 (excluding the 2021 pandemic spike). This surge occurred before the West Asia war impacted global oil markets and sent raw material costs soaring.

articles.headline: Global Stocks Slide Again as Trump’s Iran Pause Fails to Calm Markets
articles.summary: Global stock markets fell, and oil prices rose again as West Asia conflict fears persisted despite Trump's 10-day extension on Iran energy strikes. The Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq declined. Brent crude rose to $110.55/barrel. 10-year US Treasury yield hit 4.4398%, reflecting inflation fears and potential rate hikes.

articles.headline: Markets Bet on "TACO Moment" in West Asia Conflict: Chris Wood
articles.summary: Jefferies' Chris Wood suggests global markets are betting on a "Trump Always Chickens Out" (TACO) moment in the West Asia conflict. This implies Trump will backtrack on aggressive threats, as seen in his temporary pause on Iran strikes. The Nifty 50 gained 1% post-pause, but bond market volatility and stagflation risks are rising.

articles.headline: Foreign Investors Flee Indian Assets at Record Pace Amid Iran War
articles.summary: Foreign investors are withdrawing from Indian bonds and equities at a record pace, selling $12.14 billion in shares since February 28, marking the biggest monthly outflow. Net bond sales under FAR hit $1.61 billion. This flight is driven by Iran war-induced oil price surges, inflation worries, and heightened macro risks for India.

articles.headline: CBDT Extends TDS Certificate Deadline to March 31 for December Quarter
articles.summary: The CBDT extended the deadline for issuing tax deducted at source (TDS) certificates for the December quarter to March 31, 2026. This helps employers and banks grappling with a slow portal. Certificates issued by this date are timely, shielding deductors from penalties. Taxpayers should cross-check entries in Form 26AS and AIS.

articles.headline: NHAI increases toll by 1-3% on Karnataka State highways
articles.summary: Road-users in Karnataka will face a marginal toll revision of around 1-3% across key State highways, effective from April 1, 2026. While passenger vehicles, including those on the Bengaluru airport stretch, remain largely unaffected, light commercial and heavy vehicles will see an increase ranging between ₹5 and ₹25. The revised toll will be implemented at 58 of the 69 toll plazas statewide.

articles.headline: Five India-bound LPG ships anchored near Hormuz Strait
articles.summary: Five LPG carriers, all Indian flagged and bound for India, are anchored north off Dubai-Ras Al Khaimah, just southwest of the Strait of Hormuz. This comes as Iran appeared to restrict ship movement. The ships carry over 1.7 lakh tonnes of LPG, equivalent to nearly six days of India’s current import requirements.

articles.headline: FM Sitharaman rejects lockdown rumours, assures fertilizer stocks
articles.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman rejected rumours of a lockdown, stating India is actively responding to the rapidly changing situation following the West Asia war. She urged political leaders to stop fearmongering and assured that adequate stocks of fertilizers are available to farmers, emphasizing the government's commitment to avoiding burden on citizens.

articles.headline: U.S. official: India trade deal near, but pulse, tariff gaps remain
articles.summary: A U.S. official stated that the U.S. and India are "not far off" from finalising an interim trade deal, but need to bridge some negotiating gaps, such as over discussions on pulses and the staging of tariff reductions. Agriculture is a deeply sensitive issue for both countries, with India seeking to safeguard its market for this commodity.

articles.headline: Housing sales dip 7% in Jan.-March due to West Asia conflict
articles.summary: Housing sales across top-seven cities rose 9% annually to over 1.01 lakh units in January-March due to a low-base effect, but fell 7% in volume terms and 6% value-wise from the previous quarter. Real estate consultant Anarock attributed this dip to global economic uncertainties stemming from the West Asia conflict, impacting buyers' sentiments.

articles.headline: Indian coffee exports hit by U.S.-Iran war, shipments stranded
articles.summary: Around 300 containers of India’s coffee exports to West Asian and European markets are now stranded at ports or moving slowly via the Strait of Hormuz. The U.S.-Israeli war on Iran has constrained passage, throwing coffee exporters into uncertainty and escalating costs. Shipments are delayed, rerouted, or offloaded far from destinations.

articles.headline: Govt slashes excise duty on petrol and diesel to shield consumers
articles.summary: The Centre reduced excise duty on petrol by Rs 21.50 per litre and diesel by Rs 29.50 per litre to combat high crude prices. Simultaneously, a windfall tax of Rs 21.50 on ATF and Rs 13 on diesel exports was imposed. The tax adjustments are expected to result in an annual revenue loss of approximately Rs 1,500 crore.

articles.headline: CAG flags Rs 169 crore irregularities in Karnataka revenue administration
articles.summary: The Comptroller and Auditor General of India (CAG) has identified irregularities totaling Rs 169 crore in Karnataka's revenue administration for the 2023-24 fiscal. The report highlights lapses in tax assessment, under-valuation of properties, and classification of documents. It also noted that nearly 29 lakh vehicles continued to operate without valid fitness certificates.

articles.headline: Indian stock markets crash 2% as Rupee hits record low
articles.summary: The BSE Sensex plummeted 1,690 points to settle at 73,583.22, while the NSE Nifty dropped 487 points on Friday. The rupee tanked 86 paise to a record low of 94.82 against the US dollar. The selloff was triggered by geopolitical tensions in West Asia and Brent crude prices surging above $100 per barrel, leading to significant foreign institutional investor outflows.

articles.headline: SEBI operationalizes framework for retail algorithmic trading from April
articles.summary: The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) will allow retail participation in algorithmic trading through brokers starting April 1, 2026. Brokers can now host third-party algorithms or offer in-house developed strategies. The new rules mandate that every algorithm must be registered with exchanges to ensure accountability, transparency, and traceability for retail investors.

articles.headline: JLR Halts UK Plant Production Due to Parts Shortage
articles.summary: Jaguar Land Rover temporarily halted production at its Solihull plant in the UK, affecting Range Rover and Range Rover Sport lines, due to supply constraints from a parts maker. The disruption is short-term and limited in scope, with no material impact on overall operations or financial performance anticipated by Tata Motors Passenger Vehicles.

articles.headline: OnePlus Shifts to Online-Only Sales Model in India
articles.summary: Chinese smartphone brand OnePlus has informed distributors and exclusive store partners of its shift to an online-only sales model in India. This move aims to offer more competitive pricing, but has raised concerns among retailers and distributors regarding the status of pending payments.

articles.headline: War Queers Pitch for Gig Players This IPL Season
articles.summary: Restaurants and food delivery platforms are cautiously hiring temporary workers this IPL season due to LPG supply disruptions amid the Gulf war. Many are limiting menus or reducing hours. While quick commerce platforms are hiring aggressively, demand for backend kitchen roles is muted, contrasting with previous IPL seasons' broad-based growth.

articles.headline: Nifty 50 Protected from Oil Shock Due to Energy Suppliers: ISec
articles.summary: ICICI Securities states that India's benchmark Nifty is better insulated from a potential oil shock from the Gulf conflict than small-cap and mid-cap segments. This is because the Nifty has higher exposure to energy suppliers like coal, electricity, and upstream oil companies that could benefit from rising prices.

articles.headline: Powerica IPO Subscribed 1.45 Times on Final Day
articles.summary: Powerica's ₹1,100 crore initial public offer was subscribed 1.45 times on Friday, the last day of bidding. The qualified institutional investor category was subscribed 4.5 times, while retail investors subscribed 0.15 times and non-institutional investors 0.44 times.

articles.headline: NARCL Set to Acquire Kay Bouvet Engineering's Debt
articles.summary: The National Asset Reconstruction Company (NARCL) is set to acquire the ₹1,000 crore debt of specialized equipment maker Kay Bouvet Engineering. This follows no challenge bids to NARCL's ₹130 crore offer in a Swiss challenge auction, making it likely the last acquisition for the government-backed bad loan aggregator this fiscal.

articles.headline: High-End Hospitality Firms See Surge in India Expansion
articles.summary: Luxury hotel signings are significantly increasing in India, with chains like Marriott, Accor, Hilton, Hyatt, IHCL, Leela, and Oberoi expanding aggressively. 77 new luxury hotels, spanning 11,800 keys, are under active development by 2031, representing 66% of the existing luxury inventory, driven by sustainable room rates.

articles.headline: Biocon to Prioritise Overseas Semaglutide Launch, Not India
articles.summary: Biocon will delay a near-term launch of generic semaglutide in India, where the market is crowded, opting instead to prioritize overseas opportunities like Canada, Brazil, and the Middle East. Incoming CEO Shreehas Tambe stated the company aims to transform into a capability-led, innovation-driven global medicines company.

articles.headline: West Asia Conflict Hitting Aviation with Surging Costs, Route Changes
articles.summary: The West Asia conflict is severely impacting aviation, with airspace disruptions, surging fuel costs, and fare pressures forcing airlines to redraw routes. Global jet fuel prices have nearly doubled. Indian carriers like Air India and IndiGo face increased flight times (1-3 hours) on Europe/North America routes, affecting profitability and capacity.

articles.headline: West Asia Tensions Dent The Leela Palaces' Business
articles.summary: The Leela Palaces, Hotels & Resorts experienced significant cancellations and deferred bookings in March due to the West Asia conflict, particularly from UAE markets. CEO Anuraag Bhatnagar anticipates mitigation for the April-June quarter, expecting MICE, celebratory, and group travel bookings to be repurposed back to India, creating new opportunities.

articles.headline: Tata Motors shares hit 52-week low after JLR production halt
articles.summary: Tata Motors shares fell 5.6% to ₹301.10 following a production pause at Jaguar Land Rover's Solihull plant in the UK. The disruption is linked to supplier-related parts issues and is expected to last until April 8. JLR is a primary earnings driver for Tata Motors, contributing nearly 70% of consolidated revenue.

articles.headline: IPL ad rates surge 25-30% led by Connected TV demand
articles.summary: Television advertising rates for the 2026 IPL season have increased by up to 30%, driven by demand for Connected TV (CTV) slots. CTV ad rates rose 20% to ₹600 cost per mille, while linear TV rates saw a 10% hike. Advertisers are prioritizing CTV for its targeted reach and data measurability. JioStar has secured 27 sponsors including Google and Campa.

articles.headline: FMCG firms raise prices as raw material and packaging costs surge
articles.summary: FMCG companies are implementing price hikes for edible oils, paints, and packaged water as crude oil prices rise and the rupee depreciates. PET resin prices have climbed to ₹143 per kg. Players like Asian Paints and Parle Products are monitoring the situation, with second-round price increases likely if West Asian tensions persist through April.

articles.headline: Hospitality sector faces higher costs and declining tourist footfalls
articles.summary: Hotels and restaurants are struggling with a 3x increase in commercial LPG costs and a sharp drop in international tourism due to the West Asia conflict. Many establishments have reduced operating hours and menu offerings. The industry is currently in survival mode as MICE events and wedding bookings face headwinds from subdued consumer sentiment.

articles.headline: Auto sector operations remain near normal despite supply chain jitters
articles.summary: Indian automobile manufacturers report that plant operations are functioning at near-normal levels despite the ongoing conflict in West Asia. Companies are tracking logistics volatility daily and diversifying sourcing. The Ministry of Heavy Industries has suggested shifting factory operations from oil-based fuels to electricity to mitigate potential shortages.

articles.headline: Gold prices hit by volatility and shift to crude oil bets
articles.summary: Geopolitical tensions in West Asia have led to sharp swings in gold prices, which declined 15% in March as investors shifted focus to crude oil. Rupee depreciation has made imports costlier, while logistical disruptions in Dubai have hindered bullion exports. Retail jewellery demand remains cautious as consumers adjust to new price levels.

articles.headline: ICRA revises Indian aviation industry outlook to negative
articles.summary: Rating agency ICRA has downgraded its outlook for the Indian aviation industry to negative from stable. The sector is facing rising jet fuel prices, rupee depreciation, and flight cancellations due to the West Asia conflict. ICRA projects domestic passenger traffic growth for FY26 to be significantly lower at 0-3%, citing increased operating costs and demand risks.

articles.headline: Morbi ceramic units shut down 700 plants due to gas shortage
articles.summary: Over 700 ceramic units in Gujarat's Morbi cluster have suspended operations for 30 days due to gas shortages and export disruptions. Manufacturers plan to hike prices once production resumes to recover lost margins. Approximately 60% of the cluster's 4 lakh workers have returned to their villages, while ₹102 crore worth of export consignments were returned.

articles.headline: BMW 5 Series shifts focus to luxury and passenger comfort
articles.summary: The new BMW 530Li M Sport prioritizes rear-seat space and luxury over its traditional driver-centric focus. The long-wheelbase version, priced at ₹74.80 lakh, features a 2.0-litre twin-scroll turbocharged engine producing 254.7 bhp. New updates include an illuminated panoramic sunroof and an 18-speaker Bowers & Wilkins sound system.

articles.headline: Harley-Davidson Breakout 117 offers bold custom styling in India
articles.summary: The Harley-Davidson Breakout 117 is a factory-built chopper priced at ₹31 lakh. It features a 1923 cc V-twin engine and an exceptionally wide 240-section rear tyre. While it faces ergonomic challenges and heat issues in traffic, the motorcycle serves as a major style statement with 100 bhp and 17.1 kg-m of torque performance.

articles.headline: Lexus ES 500e EV launched in India at ₹89.99 lakh
articles.summary: Lexus has entered the Indian electric vehicle market with the ES 500e sedan. The eighth-generation model features a 74.7-kWh battery and dual electric motors delivering 338 bhp. It offers a claimed range of 580 km and an all-wheel-drive system. Fast charging capabilities allow the vehicle to reach 80 per cent charge in 28 minutes.

articles.headline: Bimota unveils custom KB399 based on Kawasaki Ninja
articles.summary: Italian manufacturer Bimota has introduced the KB399, a bespoke version of the Kawasaki Ninja ZX-4RR. The bike features carbon fiber bodywork, an Akrapovic exhaust, and Brembo Stylema brakes. While performance figures were not fully detailed, it draws styling inspiration from the larger KB998 Rimini and utilizes billet aluminum components.

articles.headline: Hyundai launches refreshed 2026 Exter micro-SUV in India
articles.summary: Hyundai has released the updated Exter micro-SUV with a starting price of ₹5.80 lakh. The refresh includes updated front bumper styling, beefier claddings, and new features like rear USB-C ports and a driver's seat armrest. It maintains the 1.2-litre engine with manual, AMT, and petrol-plus-CNG options, focusing on enhanced safety and connectivity.

articles.headline: Tenneco Clean Air targets acquisitions and R&D expansion in India
articles.summary: Auto component major Tenneco Clean Air India is planning acquisitions and a $2 million R&D upgrade at its Hosur facility. The zero-debt company holds a 52% market share in the Indian passenger vehicle shock absorber market. It is also building a new plant in Haryana to strengthen its presence in North India and evaluating higher-margin industrial applications.

articles.headline: Indian electronics prices set to rise 7-12% from April 1
articles.summary: Prices for televisions, air conditioners, and washing machines will increase from April 1 due to input cost inflation and supply disruptions. Hisense India CEO Pankaj Rana stated that AC prices will see the steepest hike at 10-12%, driven by rising plastic and logistics costs. The increase reflects a structural reset as manufacturers struggle to absorb mounting pressures.

articles.headline: Reliance valuation erodes by ₹87,014 crore in stock market sell-off
articles.summary: Reliance Industries shares plummeted 4.6% on Friday, leading to a massive valuation loss of ₹87,014 crore. The fall mirrored a broader bearish trend in the Indian market fueled by geopolitical tensions and a record rupee depreciation. Reliance ended the day at ₹1,348.25 on the BSE after reaching an intra-day low of ₹1,345.

articles.headline: SEBI approves six IPOs as Jindal Supreme withdraws proposal
articles.summary: Markets regulator SEBI has cleared initial public offerings for six firms, including Vishvaraj Environment (₹2,250 crore) and Symbiotec Pharmalab (₹2,180 crore). Other approvals include Prasol Chemicals, SAEL Industries, and NoPaperForms Solutions. Meanwhile, Jindal Supreme (India) withdrew its draft papers. The proceeds are largely intended for debt repayment and capacity expansion.

articles.headline: Nasdaq 100 enters correction territory as big tech shares fall
articles.summary: The Nasdaq 100 Index has fallen into correction territory, dropping 11% from its October peak. Tech giants like Nvidia, Meta, and Microsoft are facing mounting skepticism over AI spending and potential US tariffs. The index's decline reflects a risk-off sentiment among investors as earnings growth expectations for tech firms begin to normalize.

articles.headline: ACME Solar shares jump 6% on bullish brokerage commentary
articles.summary: Shares of ACME Solar Holdings rose 6% after Investec initiated coverage with a 'Buy' rating and a ₹319 target price. Analysts highlighted the company's strong execution visibility and its shift toward hybrid and storage-backed renewable projects. ACME's portfolio is increasingly aligned with round-the-clock clean power demand, which is expected to drive robust earnings growth.

articles.headline: US Treasury to launch insurance program for Hormuz shipping
articles.summary: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent announced a new insurance program through the Development Finance Corporation to boost shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. The initiative, in conjunction with Central Command, aims to provide naval escorts and security guarantees for oil tankers. The move comes amid rising concerns about historically high insurance costs during the Iran war.

articles.headline: Toyota joint ventures recall 5.6 lakh SUVs in China
articles.summary: Toyota Motor Corp's Chinese joint ventures are recalling more than 5,60,000 sport utility vehicles starting April 1. The recall addresses a defect in second-row seats that could jeopardize passenger safety. The action involves Guangzhou Automobile Group and China FAW Group, following a statement from China's market regulator.

articles.headline: Indian cotton prices rise as rupee weakens against US dollar
articles.summary: Domestic cotton prices increased by ₹300 per candy as the falling rupee made imports significantly more expensive. The Cotton Corporation of India has sold 39 lakh bales in March alone due to robust demand from spinning mills and multinational traders. ICE cotton futures have gained over 14% since early March, now hovering above 69 cents per pound.

articles.headline: LPG shortage impacts tea demand as Kerala eateries shut down
articles.summary: An ongoing commercial LPG shortage in Kerala has led to a decline in tea demand at the Kochi auction as restaurants and tea stalls remain shut. Prices for popular liquoring grades fell by ₹3-₹5 per kg. Blenders absorbed only 64% of the offered CTC dust, while exports to West Asia also saw a downward trend due to regional unrest.

articles.headline: Coir and rubber mat exporters hit by raw material supply shocks
articles.summary: Indian exporters of coir and rubber mats are facing a severe crisis due to shortages of PVC resin and high freight rates caused by the US-Israel-Iran war. Manufacturers have pre-committed orders at fixed prices but now face escalating input costs and potential order cancellations. The industry has sought urgent government intervention and temporary duty cuts.

articles.headline: Global steel production falls 2.2% in February as China output drops
articles.summary: Worldwide steel output fell to 141.8 million tonnes in February 2026, a 2.2% decrease year-on-year. Top producer China recorded a 3.6% drop, while Indian production surged 7.7% to 13.6 million tonnes. Output in Russia and the CIS region slipped 10.5%, reflecting the impact of geopolitical instability on global industrial manufacturing.

articles.headline: Biocon appoints Shreehas Tambe as CEO to lead business integration
articles.summary: Biocon Ltd named Shreehas Tambe as CEO and Managing Director, effective April 1, 2026. Tambe will oversee the integration of the biologics and generics businesses under a unified global structure. The restructuring follows the integration of Biocon Biologics and aims to sharpen focus on high-growth therapy areas like oncology and immunology.

articles.headline: India's luxury sales stall amid severe shortage of retail space
articles.summary: Rapid economic growth in India has fueled demand for high-end brands, but expansion is hampered by a lack of grade-A luxury malls. India currently has only three true luxury malls, compared to hundreds in China. Major labels like Prada and Patek Philippe have no physical stores in India, as developers project that planned luxury malls in Mumbai and Gurugram will take years to open.

articles.headline: Cleantech hiring in India surges 57% over two-year period
articles.summary: Employment in India's cleantech sector grew by 57% between 2023 and 2025, driven by massive renewable capacity additions and AI integration. A CIEL HR Services report highlighted high demand for engineers, project managers, and energy specialists. However, the sector faces a talent gap and a relatively low median tenure of 2.7 years among employees.

articles.headline: FCC proposes bringing outsourced call centre jobs back to US
articles.summary: The US Federal Communications Commission has proposed new measures to encourage American corporations to bring outsourced call center jobs back to the US. Currently, nearly 70% of US companies outsource departments to countries such as India, the Philippines, and South Africa. The plan aims to reverse a multi-decade trend of shifting customer service operations abroad.

articles.headline: Airpay Partners with MSCB to Expand UPI Payments in Rural Maharashtra
articles.summary: Airpay Payment Services collaborated with Maharashtra State Co-operative Bank (MSCB) to roll out UPI-based digital payments across MSCB's network, including Primary Agricultural Credit Societies and branches. This aims to accelerate financial inclusion in rural areas, converting a significant portion of MSCB's ₹62,000 crore annual business to digital transactions.

articles.headline: EaseMyTrip Cofounder Buys ₹15.9 Crore Penthouse in Mumbai
articles.summary: Rikant Pittie, cofounder and CEO of EaseMyTrip, acquired a 2,240 sq ft penthouse in DLF's The Westpark project in Andheri West for ₹15.9 crore. The transaction, at ~₹76,500 per sq ft carpet area, is among Mumbai's highest per square foot deals in the western suburbs.

articles.headline: Biocon MD Outlines Three-Pronged Strategy Post-Integration
articles.summary: Shreehas Tambe, Biocon's new MD & CEO, detailed a three-pronged strategy post-integration (Biocon with Biocon Biologics): people, technology, and patient access. The firm aims for capability-led growth, expanding portfolio into adjacencies like peptides and insulin, leveraging unified supply chains, and becoming a top-five global biosimilar player, eventually shaping the market.

articles.headline: Adani Wins Jaiprakash Associates Bid Over Vedanta Despite Lower NPV
articles.summary: The NCLT approved Adani Enterprises' ₹15,000 crore resolution plan for Jaiprakash Associates Ltd (JAL), dismissing Vedanta's objections. Adani's bid was favored for its faster timelines, higher upfront repayment (₹6,000 crore), and 94% CoC voting share, despite Vedanta offering a higher net present value. Experts noted the IBC process increasingly prioritizes recovery over resolution.

articles.headline: Strategic M&As Dominating India’s Deeptech Startup Exits
articles.summary: Venture capital investors predict strategic M&As will be the primary exit route for Indian deeptech startups in space, semiconductors, defence, and robotics, followed by IPOs. M&A deals rose from 5 in 2024 to 15 in 2025. Global corporations adopt a 'buy-over-build' strategy for proprietary technology. Public markets are also showing growing appetite for IP-backed deeptech stories.

articles.headline: Dr Reddy's to Rebrand Diabetes Drug "Olymviq" to "Olymra" After Ozempic Dispute
articles.summary: Dr Reddy’s Laboratories (DRL) informed the Delhi High Court of its intent to rebrand its diabetes medication "Olymviq" as "Olymra," following a trademark dispute with Novo Nordisk, which argued "Olymviq" sounded like its weight-loss drug Ozempic, causing confusion. The dispute arose after Novo Nordisk's semaglutide patent expired, allowing generics.

articles.headline: Median Non-Promoter CEO Pay Rises 5% to ₹10.5 Crore in FY26
articles.summary: Deloitte's report states median compensation for non-promoter CEOs rose 5% to ₹10.5 crore in FY26, the slowest growth since Covid-19. CFOs saw the highest increase due to high attrition, focus on capital efficiency, and direct shareholder accountability. Market volatility and geopolitical risks are expected to temper future pay increases.

articles.headline: Luxury Travel Segment Resilient Despite West Asia War: Leela Hotels CEO
articles.summary: Anuraag Bhatnagar, CEO of Leela Palaces Hotels & Resorts, stated that while the West Asia war caused some cancellations and deferrals, the luxury travel segment remains resilient. Travellers are shifting focus to other regions, including domestic India, leading to a quick recovery. Post-Covid, travel habits have changed, with strong bounce-back noted.

articles.headline: BYD's Annual Profit Plunges 19%, First Drop in Four Years
articles.summary: China’s biggest electric automaker, BYD, reported a steeper-than-expected 19% fall in net profit to 32.6 billion yuan ($4.72 billion) for the first time in four years. Revenue growth was its weakest in six years (3.5%), and the workforce was cut by 10.2%. The company chairman acknowledged "brutal 'knockout stage'" competition.

articles.headline: NSE Nifty Reits & Realty Index Paves Way for Dedicated Reit MFs
articles.summary: NSE's launch of the Nifty Reits & Realty Index brings India's first Reit-focused mutual fund schemes closer. Fund houses are evaluating passive products for launch in H2 2026, following Sebi's reclassification of Reits as equity. The 15-stock index includes listed Reits (64% weight) and realty stocks, offering 3.3% dividend yield.

articles.headline: Infosys Acquisitions Bolster Healthcare, Insurance; Eye AI-Driven Services
articles.summary: Infosys acquired Optimum Healthcare IT ($465M) and Stratus Global ($95M) for $560M cash, adding 2,050 staff. These strengthen healthcare and P&C insurance capabilities by integrating Infosys' AI Topaz and Cobalt platforms. Expected to boost FY27 revenue by 1.2-2.25%, acquisitions align with Infosys' AI leadership strategy, with 90% workforce AI-trained.

articles.headline: ICICI Prudential MF Reclassifies Thematic Advantage FoF
articles.summary: ICICI Prudential Mutual Fund announced reclassification of its "ICICI Prudential Thematic Advantage Fund (FOF)" to "ICICI Prudential Aggressive Hybrid Active FOF," effective April 1, 2026. This change complies with Sebi’s framework for fund of funds, aiming to combine sector and theme rotation.

articles.headline: DBS Enters India ECM with Manipal Health's $1 Billion IPO Mandate
articles.summary: DBS Group Holdings Ltd entered India’s equity capital market (ECM) by securing its first mandate from Manipal Health Enterprises for its upcoming $1 billion IPO. Temasek Holdings is a major shareholder in both DBS and Manipal Health. India's fundraising activity reached over $22 billion last year, ranking fourth globally.

articles.headline: Three IPOs Successfully Close Amid Market Volatility
articles.summary: Three IPOs—Powerica (1.5x subscribed), Sai Parenterals (fully subscribed), and Amir Chand Jagdish Kumar (3.4x subscribed)—closed successfully, raising nearly ₹2,000 crore despite secondary market turbulence. March saw nine IPOs mobilize ₹11,852 crore, indicating resilience in primary market activity.

articles.headline: Sebi Approves Six IPOs, Including Vishvaraj Environment and SAEL Industries
articles.summary: Market regulator Sebi issued final observations this week on the draft red herring prospectuses (DRHPs) for six companies. Approved firms include Vishvaraj Environment, a water utility and wastewater management developer, and SAEL Industries, a renewable energy company, indicating pipeline for future public listings.

articles.headline: Export Duty on Fuel Hits Reliance Industries' Margins
articles.summary: Reliance Industries Ltd (RIL) shares fell 4.5% after the government reimposed export duties on petrol, diesel, and aviation turbine fuel. This move raises concerns over refining margins and profitability, as RIL exports a significant share of its output to global markets. Analysts expect short-term volatility despite positive long-term views.

articles.headline: GSK Velu: Building a Multi-Vertical Healthcare Powerhouse
articles.summary: GSK Velu, a serial healthcare entrepreneur, details his journey from a childhood accident to building companies like Trivitron Healthcare, Neuberg Diagnostics, and Maxivision Eye Hospitals. He plans to list Neuberg, Maxivision, and Trivitron, aiming for Neuberg to be India’s second-largest diagnostic chain. He emphasizes integrating AI into healthcare.

articles.headline: West Asia War Fuels Crisis, Strains E-commerce Supply Chains
articles.summary: India's e-commerce and quick-commerce sectors are feeling the West Asia energy crisis. Fuel shortages disrupt dark stores, while tighter diesel credit terms squeeze last-mile logistics. LPG shortages delay fulfilment centres, increasing costs and slowing infrastructure expansion. Experts predict consumer spending curbs and price hikes across categories, shifting focus to cost discipline.

articles.headline: Indian indices Nifty, Sensex drop 2% as crude oil rises to $111/bbl
articles.summary: Benchmark indices Nifty50 and Sensex shed 2.09% and 2.25% respectively, closing at 22,819.60 and 73,583.22. This decline occurred after crude oil prices rebounded to $111 a barrel, as hopes of de-escalation between the U.S. and Iran faded. Foreign Institutional Investors (FIIs) withdrew ₹1,565.63 crore from the equity market.

articles.headline: Federation claims renewed liquor licences included in e-auction
articles.summary: The Federation of Karnataka Wines Merchants Association alleged that the Excise Department included 86 already-renewed liquor licences as part of the 579 licences to be e-auctioned. Demanding a probe into this, the federation stated it would approach the Governor and Enforcement Directorate with a complaint, arguing that currently operational licenses are being auctioned.

articles.headline: Automakers told to optimize output amid fuel shortage fears
articles.summary: India has asked automakers and parts suppliers to tighten production schedules to conserve fuel. This measure comes amid fears of shortages caused by disrupted oil and gas imports from the Gulf due to the Iran war, according to a government memo seen by Reuters.

articles.headline: Gowri Kailasam honored for driving excellence in industry
articles.summary: Gowri Kailasam, CEO of Rane (Madras) Limited, was honored with The Hindu Excellence in Business Leadership Award. Starting her career at Ford Motor Company, she rose to leadership at Rane, guiding the company to both the Deming Prize and the Deming Grand Prize. She serves as Chairwoman of Indian Women Network, Tamil Nadu.

articles.headline: India and Russia to restart LNG trade amid Gulf crisis
articles.summary: India and Russia have agreed to resume direct sales of Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG) for the first time since the start of the Ukraine war. The move follows negotiations between Indian diplomats and Russian gas officials to secure energy supplies amid the shutdown of the Strait of Hormuz. A charter operator for Russian LNG vessels is expected to start operations within a month.

articles.headline: PM Modi chairs Chief Ministers' meeting on West Asia conflict impact
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi reviewed the supply of essential goods, fuel, and gas with state Chief Ministers. Emphasizing a 'Team India' approach, he called for coordination to manage supply chain disruptions caused by the West Asia war. The government has operationalized an inter-ministerial group to monitor the situation daily and ensure economic stability and energy security.

articles.headline: Congress alleges Centre extracted ₹43 crore daily in fuel taxes
articles.summary: AICC general secretary Randeep Singh Surjewala accused the Central government of collecting ₹43 crore daily through fuel taxes over the last 11 years. The Congress claimed the recent excise duty cut would benefit oil companies rather than consumers. Surjewala suggested that fuel prices might rise again after the upcoming state elections on April 9 and 23.

articles.headline: AIADMK releases candidate names for 127 more Tamil Nadu seats
articles.summary: The AIADMK announced its second list of 127 candidates for the April 23 Tamil Nadu Assembly elections. The list includes several former ministers and sitting MLAs. General secretary Edappadi K Palaniswami has now named candidates for 150 constituencies. The party is leading the NDA alliance in the state and will contest alongside the BJP, PMK, and AMMK.

articles.headline: K Kavitha announces launch of regional party 'Telangana Praja Jagruthi'
articles.summary: Telangana Jagruthi president K Kavitha announced the formation of a new regional party with the goal of achieving 'social justice' in the state. The official launch, including the unveiling of the flag and agenda, is scheduled for April 25 at a rally in Medchal-Malkajgiri. Kavitha stated the party will prioritize representation for women, youth, and marginalized communities.

articles.headline: Ram Navami rally clashes leave 20 injured in West Bengal
articles.summary: At least 20 people were injured during communal violence at a Ram Navami rally in Murshidabad's Raghunathganj. Prohibitory orders under Section 163 of BNSS were imposed in the town. The Election Commission sought a report from the state CEO and ordered the suspension of a police official for negligence. The incident sparked political blame between the BJP and TMC.

articles.headline: Defence Ministry Approves `2.4 Lakh Crore Procurement Deals
articles.summary: The Defence Acquisition Council cleared major procurement deals worth over ₹2.38 lakh crore, including unmanned strike aircraft, S-400 air defence systems, transport aircraft, and artillery guns. This includes a ₹1 lakh crore deal for 60 military transport aircraft to be made in India with foreign assistance, seeing keen private sector competition.

articles.headline: PM Modi Urges Team India Synergy with CMs Amid West Asia Crisis
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi held a virtual meeting with chief ministers, urging a 'Team India' approach to address challenges from the West Asia conflict. He emphasized vigilance against hoarding, black marketing, and misinformation, while ensuring no disruption to supply chains, essential supplies, and engagement with industry.

articles.headline: India Slams Pakistan's Double Standards on Global Conflicts
articles.summary: India criticized Pakistan's stance on global conflicts, citing remarks by its leaders including Defence Minister Khwaja Asif, who admitted doing 'dirty work for the West.' MEA spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal also highlighted Pakistan's denial of 1971 atrocities in Bangladesh and its systemic victimisation of minorities.

articles.headline: J&K Assembly Uproar Over Iran War, Calls for PM's Intervention
articles.summary: The Jammu and Kashmir Assembly saw uproar over the West Asia crisis, with CM Omar Abdullah condemning it as an 'unjust and illegal war' and urging PM Modi to intervene. Ruling and opposition legislators expressed solidarity with Iran, while BJP MLAs deemed the issue international and beyond the Assembly's remit.

articles.headline: India Orders Tunguska Air Defence Missiles from Russia for Drone Threats
articles.summary: Amid rising drone threats, India has placed new orders for Tunguska air defence missiles from Russia, worth ₹445 crore. This revives and upgrades a legacy system now adapted for counter-unmanned warfare, enhancing multilayered air defence against low-flying drones and cruise missiles, complementing systems like the S-400.

articles.headline: India, Europe Eye Deeper Ties with Modi's Upcoming Visits
articles.summary: India and Europe are set to strengthen partnerships following the India-EU free trade agreement. PM Narendra Modi plans visits to Oslo for the India-Nordic Summit and Italy in mid-May, and will attend the G-7 Summit in France in mid-June. These visits aim to consolidate collaborations amid global supply chain stress.

articles.headline: Congress Rebel Withdraws from Davanagere South Bypoll
articles.summary: Congress rebel candidate Sadique Pailwan withdrew from the Davanagere South bypoll in favour of official candidate Samarth Shamanur. This follows negotiations by Congress leaders, including CM Siddaramaiah and Dy CM DK Shivakumar, to prevent a split of minority votes in the constituency, where Muslims form a majority.

articles.headline: ECI to Release Second Supplementary Voter List in West Bengal
articles.summary: The Election Commission of India will publish West Bengal's second supplementary voter list by Friday night, after judicial magistrates' e-signing. A three-member committee is formed to address infrastructure for appellate tribunals. This follows disposal of 37 lakh cases by 705 judicial officers under Calcutta HC monitoring.

articles.headline: KCR’s Daughter Kavitha to Launch New Political Party
articles.summary: Former MP Kavitha Kalvakuntla, daughter of K Chandrasekhara Rao, announced the launch of her political party, 'Sarvodaya Telangana,' in Nizamabad. The official launch is scheduled for April 25 in Medchal, pitching herself as a 'Telangana Adabidda' and advocating for a new, inclusive political force led by a woman.

articles.headline: ECI Organises Cyclothon, Tram Ride for Voter Awareness in Bengal
articles.summary: The ECI launched a Cyclothon from Howrah Bridge and a special Kolkata tramcar ride, featuring Chhota Bheem and Chutki, to promote voter awareness in West Bengal. The 'Chunav Ka Parv, Paschim Bengal Ka Garv' campaign integrates local cultural heritage to make the electoral process inclusive and engaging, especially for young voters.

articles.headline: WTO DG Calls Meeting with Goyal 'Very Tough', Seeks Common Ground
articles.summary: WTO Director-General Ngozi Okonjo-Iweala described her meeting with Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal as 'very tough' but expressed hope for common ground. They deliberated on WTO reforms, with India reiterating its demand for consensus-based decision-making and opposing proposals to include plurilateral outcomes like Investment Facilitation for Development in the WTO framework.

articles.headline: Parliament completes budgetary exercise for fiscal year 2026-27
articles.summary: The Rajya Sabha returned the Finance Bill to the Lok Sabha on Friday, marking the completion of the budgetary exercise for FY 2026-27. During the session, Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman addressed the impact of the West Asia conflict and the government's strategy to manage fiscal deficit while shielding consumers from rising global energy costs.

articles.headline: PM Modi urges States to take strict measures against hoarding
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi directed states to implement strict measures against hoarding and profiteering in response to the West Asia conflict. During a video conference with Chief Ministers, he emphasized the need for administrative alertness to prevent supply chain disruptions and urged states to accelerate the adoption of alternative energy sources like biofuels.

articles.headline: Kerala Opposition leader Satheesan unveils alternative economic plan
articles.summary: Kerala's Leader of the Opposition VD Satheesan announced that the UDF is ready with an economic plan featuring 27 aviation projects and 1,000 multi-sector projects. He criticized the current LDF government for the state's financial crisis and high out-of-pocket healthcare expenses. Satheesan proposed a new tax administration and the channelization of NRI remittances.

articles.headline: Jose Charles Martin to contest Kamaraj Nagar in Puducherry polls
articles.summary: Jose Charles Martin, son of lottery baron Santiago Martin, will contest the Kamaraj Nagar constituency in Puducherry for the Latchiya Jananayaga Katchi (LJK). Allied with the NDA, Martin disclosed total assets worth ₹597 crore in his election affidavit. His campaign focuses on development and business-oriented efficiency to transform Puducherry into a global hub.

articles.headline: Kerala sees wave of veteran legislators switching political loyalties
articles.summary: The lead-up to the 2026 Kerala Assembly elections is marked by an unusual wave of defections, primarily from the LDF. Six former legislators, including three-term MLA Aisha Potty and former minister G Sudhakaran, have either joined the BJP, Congress, or are contesting as independents. Analysts describe the trend as a significant shift in the state's traditionally stable cadre politics.

articles.headline: AIADMK renominates 30 sitting MLAs in second candidate list
articles.summary: The AIADMK has released its second list of candidates for the upcoming Tamil Nadu elections, renominating 30 sitting MLAs. The party has now announced 150 of its 169 total contestants. The second list includes 17 women, including Leema Rose Martin. Notable sitting MLAs renominatied include Pollachi Jayaraman and Dalavai Sundaram.

articles.headline: Actor Vijay’s TVK alleges Chennai Police denied rally permission
articles.summary: Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) claimed the Chennai Police denied permission for actor Vijay's Perambur rally on Saturday. The party described the obstruction as a fascist attack on democracy and a sign of DMK's political fear. Authorities cited an unsuitable location and a dug-up trench at the venue as reasons for the denial.

articles.headline: Agencies launch Kerala-wide crackdown on unaccounted election cash
articles.summary: A joint enforcement effort by the ED, Income Tax, and other agencies has intensified vehicle checks in Kerala to curb the movement of unaccounted cash. Over ₹2.1 crore in cash has been seized so far, with gold and contraband together estimated to exceed ₹30 crore. Personnel from outside the state have been deployed to assist flying squads and static surveillance teams.

articles.headline: War of words erupts between Congress and CPI(M) in Kerala
articles.summary: Political tensions in Kerala have escalated as AICC General Secretary KC Venugopal accused Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan of being wary of Rahul Gandhi’s influence. Venugopal alleged that Vijayan targets Gandhi to avoid criticizing PM Modi due to a secret understanding with the Centre on issues like labour codes and funds.

articles.headline: Finance Minister Sitharaman dismisses speculation about impending lockdown
articles.summary: Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman strongly dismissed rumors of a lockdown due to the West Asia war, labeling them as fear-mongering. She noted that while neighboring Pakistan has announced 'smart lockdowns' and staff shifts to conserve fuel, India is managing its fiscal stance carefully without imposing restrictions on movement or office operations.

articles.headline: PM Modi Meets CMs, Centre Forms Informal Group on West Asia War Impact
articles.summary: PM Narendra Modi reviewed states' preparedness for the West Asia war impact with CMs (excluding poll-bound states), stressing a 'Team India' approach. An informal group of ministers (IGoM), led by Defence Minister Rajnath Singh, was formed to monitor the conflict's national impact, including on oil, fertilisers, and supply chains.

articles.headline: Finance Commission Urges CAG to Ensure Uniformity in State Subsidy Reporting
articles.summary: The Sixteenth Finance Commission asked the CAG to enforce "uniform and comparable" reporting of state subsidies in Finance Accounts, regardless of how states classify them. This is crucial for fiscal stability and borrowing limit assessments, especially as off-budget borrowings are now considered. The Commission also called for public disclosure of CAG's tax devolution certification.

articles.headline: India Sends Humanitarian Aid to Iran Amid Strait of Hormuz Standoff
articles.summary: As India seeks Iran's help for 20 merchant vessels to exit the Strait of Hormuz, New Delhi has dispatched humanitarian aid, including lifesaving medicines, to Tehran. Iran has so far allowed four Indian LPG-carrying vessels to transit on a case-by-case basis. India is also working to forge a consensus within the BRICS grouping on the conflict.

articles.headline: Defence Acquisition Council Approves ₹2.38 Trillion Defence Proposals
articles.summary: The Defence Acquisition Council (DAC), chaired by Defence Minister Rajnath Singh, approved defence proposals worth ₹2.38 trillion, focusing on strengthening air defence. Key approvals include S-400 Long Range Surface-to-Air Missile systems, Air Defence Tracked System for the Army, Medium Transport Aircraft, and Remotely Piloted Strike Aircraft for the Air Force.

articles.headline: Seven Panels to Brief Informal GoM on West Asia Crisis Response
articles.summary: Seven empowered groups of secretaries are expected to brief an informal group of ministers (IGoM), led by Defence Minister Rajnath Singh, on measures to address the West Asia crisis. The groups will assess risks to energy supplies, trade, supply chains, and logistics, aiming to mitigate disruptions and identify alternative import sources.

articles.headline: G7 Foreign Ministers Meet Amid Wars, US Struggles to Align Allies
articles.summary: G7 foreign ministers met in France to discuss West Asia and Ukraine conflicts. Divisions were apparent over the US-Israel war with Iran, with allies sceptical of US strategy following Trump’s criticisms of NATO. France and Britain reiterated calls for diplomatic solutions, highlighting differences on offensive actions.

articles.headline: Congress rebel Sadiq Pahilwan withdraws from Davangere bypoll
articles.summary: Congress rebel candidate Sadiq Pahilwan retired from the electoral contest in the byelection to the Davangere South Assembly constituency, scheduled for April 9. This decision followed intervention by Chief Minister Siddaramaiah and Deputy Chief Minister D.K. Shivakumar. Pahilwan announced his full support for Congress candidate Samarth Mallikarjun.

articles.headline: Tamil Nadu Assembly poll will be close, DMK to form government: Chidambaram
articles.summary: Congress veteran P. Chidambaram stated that the upcoming Tamil Nadu Assembly election will be a close contest between two strong fronts, unlike the one-sided 2024 Lok Sabha poll. He predicted that the DMK will form the government, but cautioned that the BJP will use every tactic to influence the election. He also noted the AIADMK seems compelled to align with the BJP's agenda.

articles.headline: Central Bengaluru corporation's maiden budget focuses on healthcare
articles.summary: The Central City Corporation presented its first budget for 2026-27 with a total outlay of ₹3,426.60 crore, projecting a marginal surplus. Key focuses include healthcare expansion with bike ambulances and upgrading maternity hospitals, road infrastructure development (over 60% of budget), and market redevelopment. The 'Arogya Mitra' initiative earmarks ₹19 crore for Smart Virtual Clinics.

articles.headline: East Bengaluru corporation presents maiden ₹3,889.98 crore budget
articles.summary: Bengaluru East City Corporation Commissioner D. S. Ramesh presented the corporation’s first annual budget with a total outlay of ₹3,889.98 crore, prioritizing connectivity, infrastructure, and public health. A significant 62% of the total outlay is allocated for development works. The corporation identified 58 flood-prone areas, addressing 38 and allocating funds for the remainder.

articles.headline: Central Bengaluru corporation imposes 2% tax on new Khatas
articles.summary: The Bengaluru Central City Corporation introduced a new 2% tax on stamp duty for issuing new Khatas in its budget, aiming to boost revenue and widen the property tax base. For a ₹60 lakh property, the new rule would impose a ₹7,200 charge. Clarification is still needed on whether this applies to Khata transfers and divisions.

articles.headline: Congress calls fuel price cut a ploy, warns of post-poll hike
articles.summary: Karnataka's ruling Congress termed the Centre's reduction in excise duty on petroleum fuel a mere "balance sheet management" and "convenient accounting" to benefit oil companies. Chief Minister Siddaramaiah, Deputy CM D.K. Shivakumar, and AICC General Secretary Randeep S. Surjewala warned of an imminent hike in fuel prices after the upcoming Assembly elections.

articles.headline: BJP slams Karnataka CM for skipping PM's video conference
articles.summary: BJP State president B.Y. Vijayendra criticized Chief Minister Siddaramaiah for not attending a video conference with Prime Minister Narendra Modi, stating it reflected his "innate anti-Modi attitude." Food and Civil Supplies Minister K.H. Muniyappa was nominated to attend the meeting instead.

articles.headline: BJP president B.Y. Vijayendra opposes MLAs seeking IPL tickets
articles.summary: BJP State president B.Y. Vijayendra stated his personal opposition to MLAs asking for IPL tickets. He emphasized that legislators are not bigger or different from the common man and should not be seeking special privileges, especially with the recent tragedy where 11 fans died during RCB victory celebrations.

articles.headline: Cong. leader alleges software project scam in Kerala Cooperation Dept.
articles.summary: Senior Congress leader Ramesh Chennithala alleged a major scam in the Kerala Cooperation department linked to the development of a 'uniform software solution' for 4,415 PACS branches. He claimed the original ₹206 crore contract to TCS was cancelled, and a fresh tender was floated under "mysterious circumstances" favoring a Kannur-based firm.

articles.headline: PM Modi stresses Centre-States cooperation amid West Asia war
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi on Friday stressed the need for coordination between the Centre and States to overcome the supply chain crisis caused by the West Asia war. He called for a "Team India" effort to ensure the supply of fuel and fertilizers, and a crackdown on hoarders and rumour-mongers, emphasizing the importance of public confidence.

articles.headline: Derek O'Brien questions tacit govt-EC agreement on CEC removal
articles.summary: Trinamool Congress Rajya Sabha leader Derek O’Brien questioned a "tacit understanding" between the government and the Chief Election Commissioner (CEC) as notices for CEC Gyanesh Kumar's removal, submitted by 193 Opposition parliamentarians, have not been acknowledged on the floor of Parliament.
