He accused the government of making a "mockery of Parliament."

articles.headline: Owaisi to campaign for AIUDF in Assam Assembly polls
articles.summary: All India Majlis-e-Ittehadul Muslimeen (AIMIM) chief Asaduddin Owaisi will campaign for the minority-based All India United Democratic Front (AIUDF) in Assam. This marks Mr. Owaisi’s first election-related visit to the State, where AIUDF is contesting 30 of Assam’s 126 Assembly seats, predominantly in Muslim-dominated areas, to consolidate minority votes.

articles.headline: Mamata Banerjee targets Election Commission over SIR list
articles.summary: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee lashed out at the Election Commission and the BJP for "not putting out in the public domain the full and final supplementary list" of voters for the special intensive revision exercise. She called it a "murder of democracy," alleging that around 40% of the 32 lakh voters scrutinized were removed from the rolls.

articles.headline: PM Modi to address Assam people via NaMo app
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will interact with the people of Assam through the NaMo application on issues related to the coming Assembly election in the State. The Assam BJP announced that Mr. Modi will directly engage with party workers and common people on March 30 at 1 p.m. via the app.

articles.headline: Kerala witnesses rise of actor-politicians in electoral arena
articles.summary: Kerala politics is seeing an increasing number of actor-politicians, with various political fronts banking on the popularity of stars to win difficult contests. Unlike in previous years, superstars Mammootty and Mohanlal are prominently involved in political conversations. While some, like K.B. Ganesh Kumar, have found success, others have tasted defeat, sparking debate on such shortcuts.

articles.headline: Bangladesh Foreign Minister to visit India next month for bilateral reset
articles.summary: Bangladesh Foreign Minister Khalilur Rahman is expected to visit India in the first week of April, marking the first high-level outreach from Bangladesh since Prime Minister Tarique Rahman came to power. The visit aims to restart bilateral ties that had cooled during the 15-month tenure of the interim government, focusing on energy security and renewal of the Ganga Waters Treaty.

articles.headline: Election Commission orders FIR after poll staffer assaulted in Bengal
articles.summary: The Election Commission has ordered an FIR and suspended a police inspector after a poll staffer was allegedly assaulted by a group in West Bengal's Nadia district. The assault occurred after the official questioned a government advertisement carrying the CM's image while the Model Code of Conduct was in force. The EC has demanded a detailed report on the incident.

articles.headline: Defence Council approves ₹2.38 lakh crore boost for S-400 systems
articles.summary: The Defence Acquisition Council (DAC) approved military upgrades worth ₹2.38 lakh crore. Key acquisitions include five additional Russian S-400 Triumf air defence units, remotely piloted strike aircraft, Dhanush artillery guns, and medium transport aircraft. The boost aims to enhance the Indian Air Force's ability to track and engage long-range vectors and drones.

articles.headline: DGCA issues strict new safety rules for VIP flights
articles.summary: The Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) mandated new protocols for flights carrying high-ranking constitutional and political figures. Operators must use twin-engine aircraft with two-person crews and avoid 'commercial pressure' from VIPs to ignore weather risks. Violations may lead to the cancellation of pilot licenses and operator permits following a recent crash in Baramati.

articles.headline: Delhi government signs MoU with Prasar Bharti for new Film City
articles.summary: The Delhi government and Prasar Bharti signed an agreement to develop an integrated film, television, and media hub in Khampur near Narela. The project aims to provide state-of-the-art production facilities and boost the media industry in the national capital. The hub will serve as a centralized location for film and TV production within the city outskirts.

articles.headline: India ramps up LPG output by 40% to combat energy crisis
articles.summary: India has increased domestic LPG production to a record 50,000 tonnes daily, a 40% surge, by invoking the Essential Commodities Act. The government is also pushing 60 lakh consumers to migrate to piped natural gas (PNG) to reduce import reliance. Despite Brent crude prices averaging $125.7, the Centre maintains 74 days of strategic reserve capacity.

articles.headline: Japan commits ₹16,420 crore ODA loan for Indian infrastructure projects
articles.summary: Japan has agreed to provide ¥275.8 billion in Official Development Assistance loans for major projects in Punjab, Karnataka, and Maharashtra. The funding covers Bengaluru Metro Phase 3, Mumbai Metro Line 11, and tertiary healthcare delivery in Maharashtra. The loans aim to mitigate traffic congestion, improve medical care quality, and promote sustainable horticulture in Punjab.

articles.headline: DGCA mandates safety norms to prevent pressure on VVIP flight pilots
articles.summary: The DGCA has introduced new regulations for VVIP flights to ensure pilots are not subjected to external pressure regarding diversions or last-minute changes. Following a fatal air crash in Baramati, charter operators must now coordinate VIP requirements through company management only. Pamphlets must be kept in aircraft indicating the sound judgment of the flight crew is final.

articles.headline: Jewar Airport to Boost Land Values in Yamuna Expressway Region
articles.summary: The Noida International Airport inauguration is expected to raise residential and industrial property prices in micro-markets along the Yamuna Expressway and surrounding Noida/Greater Noida. Plots are projected to rise 28%, apartments 22% in two years in the Yeida region. This growth is driven by planned sectors, infrastructure, and job creation from upcoming technology and film hubs.

articles.headline: Bengaluru corporations channel FAR, ad revenue to B-SMILE
articles.summary: Both Bengaluru City East and Central Corporations will transfer revenue from Premium Floor Area Ratio (FAR) and advertisements to Bengaluru-Smart Infrastructure Ltd. (B-SMILE) for various projects like tunnel roads, elevated corridors, and Sancharayukta. The East corporation will transfer full revenue, while the Central corporation will transfer 50% of these revenues.

articles.headline: Bengaluru corporations allocate major budget share to road infrastructure
articles.summary: Bengaluru's East and Central Corporations have allocated a major chunk of their maiden budgets—62% and 63% respectively—for development works, with a central focus on improving road infrastructure and addressing connectivity woes. Both corporations intend to revive road widening projects and are raising ₹200 crore each through municipal bonds exclusively for road development.

articles.headline: DGCA allows pilots to refuse VVIP flights for safety reasons
articles.summary: The Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) has issued guidelines permitting pilots flying VVIPs to refuse to take off if they believe conditions are unsafe. The rules apply to flights carrying PMs, CMs, and other high-ranking officials. The move is intended to prevent pilots from feeling pressured into flying during poor weather or with technical issues.

articles.headline: Karnataka power consumption hits record peak amid LPG crunch
articles.summary: Power consumption in Karnataka reached an all-time high peak load of 18,986 MW on March 13, driven by a shortage of LPG causing households to switch to electric stoves. The state expects demand to reach 20,000 MW as summer peaks. Officials are monitoring the situation to ensure adequate supply from hydel reservoirs and thermal stations.

articles.headline: East Bengaluru Corporation maps Rs 1.7k-crore for infrastructure projects
articles.summary: The newly split East Bengaluru Corporation has allocated Rs 1,760 crore for infrastructure, including road resurfacing, flyovers, and storm drain maintenance. Key projects include a Rs 150-crore elevated road at Varthur-Gunjur and Rs 298 crore for arterial road improvements. The budget emphasizes urban design, climate resilience, and decentralized waste management.

articles.headline: Prime Minister Modi inaugurates Phase I of Noida International Airport
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated the first phase of the Noida International Airport at Jewar, Uttar Pradesh. Developed at a cost of Rs 11,200 crore, the greenfield project aims to handle 1.20 crore passengers annually. The airport features a multi-modal cargo hub and a terminal designed to reflect the cultural heritage of Varanasi and Haridwar.

articles.headline: Seven killed in massive avalanche at Zojila Pass in Ladakh
articles.summary: An avalanche struck the Srinagar-Leh highway at Zojila Pass on Friday, killing at least seven people. Multiple passenger vehicles were buried under snow at Captain-turn. Personnel from the police, SDRF, Army, and BRO launched a rescue operation, recovering five bodies so far. Seven injured individuals were evacuated to hospitals while rescue efforts continued in difficult terrain.

articles.headline: ALPA India Urges Centralised Risk Analysis for Flights in Conflict Zones
articles.summary: Pilots' grouping ALPA India urged the civil aviation ministry to suspend flights into high-risk conflict zones until a centralised risk assessment is performed. Amid the West Asia crisis, it also sought mandatory war-risk insurance disclosure for crews operating near conflict areas, citing airlines' lack of geopolitical risk assessment capabilities.

articles.headline: DGCA Directs Pilots to Resist Pressure on VVIP Flights for Safety
articles.summary: The DGCA instructed pilots of VVIP flights to prioritize safety and refuse flights if conditions are unsafe, following a Learjet crash in January killing Maharashtra's Deputy CM. Pilots must not face "undue pressure" from VVIPs or staff, with changes routed through management. Mandatory twin-engine aircraft, two pilots, IFR equipment, and pre-flight inspections are now required.

articles.headline: Lorry crashes into car, sets off blaze; one dead
articles.summary: A late-night accident involving a steel-laden lorry and a CNG-powered car near Kurubarahalli, Bengaluru, triggered a massive fire, resulting in the death of the cab driver, Ambadas (23). The lorry driver, Sharma, sustained minor injuries. Fire and emergency services battled the flames for several hours, facing difficulty retrieving the charred body.

articles.headline: Four from Raichur district drown in Tungabhadra
articles.summary: Four people, including two women, from Manvi taluk in Raichur district, Karnataka, drowned in the Tungabhadra river near Kandkur village, Kosigi Mandal, in Kurnool district of Andhra Pradesh on Friday. The deceased were identified as 35-year-old Dasari Umavathi, 29-year-old Dasari Ratnamma, 20-year-old Dasari Shivanand, and 28-year-old Mahantesh.

articles.headline: 59 school bus drivers booked for drunk driving in Bengaluru
articles.summary: Bengaluru city traffic police booked 59 school vehicle drivers for driving under the influence of alcohol during a special early morning drive on Friday. This raises serious concerns about student safety and indicates a potential risk for schoolchildren commuting in these vehicles.

articles.headline: KSRTC bus catches fire near Lulu Mall, Bengaluru; no injuries
articles.summary: A KSRTC bus carrying 21 passengers and crew caught fire near Lulu Mall in Bengaluru late on Thursday night. Passers-by alerted the driver, who immediately halted the vehicle and evacuated all passengers safely. Fire and emergency services doused the flames, reporting no injuries or casualties.

articles.headline: Man kills brother-in-law, surrenders with body in Bengaluru
articles.summary: Srinivas, 30, allegedly bludgeoned his brother-in-law Ranjith, 24, to death and later walked into Bannerghatta Police Station carrying the victim’s body to surrender. Tensions had reportedly been brewing between the two after Ranjith was allegedly in a relationship with Srinivas’ married younger sister. Police have arrested the accused.

articles.headline: Five dead as speeding car hits auto-rickshaw in Gwalior
articles.summary: Five persons were killed after the auto-rickshaw they were traveling in was hit by a speeding car in Gwalior district of Madhya Pradesh on Friday. The incident took place around 3 a.m. in Thatipur town. The auto-rickshaw was carrying nine members of the same family. Two people have been arrested.

articles.headline: DGCA warns pilots against 'VIP pressure' during elections
articles.summary: The Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) issued a new order revising norms for flying dignitaries and election operations, telling pilots to "Say no to VIP pressure." The directive emphasizes that operational decisions must remain free from "external pressure," particularly concerning weather, visibility, and sunset operations, which pilots frequently report facing.

articles.headline: Yogita Bhayana honored for mission to eliminate sexual violence
articles.summary: Yogita Bhayana, founder of People Against Rapes in India (PARI), received The Hindu Contribution to Society Award. She has worked tirelessly on advocacy, legal support, and policy reform for survivors of sexual violence, coming into national focus after the 2012 Nirbhaya case. Her campaign helped lower the juvenile justice age from 18 to 16 years in rape cases.

articles.headline: Sheelu Francis honored for championing rural women's rights
articles.summary: Sheelu Francis received The Hindu Excellence in Agriculture and Rural Development Award for her pioneering work in empowering rural women. In 1994, she founded the Women’s Collective in Tamil Nadu to secure rights for the most marginalized. Over three decades, her collective has expanded across 19 districts, mobilizing over 1,00,000 women in various initiatives.

articles.headline: Anuja Amin honored for dedication to child safety cause
articles.summary: Anuja Amin received The Hindu Influencer Award for founding Circles of Safety, a social enterprise dedicated to preventing child sexual abuse. Through structured workshops and a rights-based curriculum, her organization reached almost 15,000 children and 10,000 adults, enabling conversations around body safety, consent, and healthy relationships.

articles.headline: Cab driver charred to death in Bengaluru after truck collision
articles.summary: A 23-year-old cab driver, Ambadas Prakash, died after his CNG-powered MUV was hit by a truck in north-west Bengaluru. The impact caused a fire that spread rapidly due to the gas cylinder, trapping the driver. The truck driver, who sustained minor injuries, has been detained. The fire also damaged two nearby parked vehicles and a scrap godown.

articles.headline: Seven killed in avalanche at Zoji La pass in Kashmir
articles.summary: At least seven people were killed and five others injured when an avalanche struck several vehicles at the Zoji La pass on the Srinagar-Leh highway. Rescue teams from the Army and police recovered seven bodies, while two people remain missing. The pass had recently been opened for traffic after winter snow clearance.

articles.headline: Allahabad High Court rules live-in relationship with married man not crime
articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court stated that a married man living with a consenting adult woman is not in breach of any law. The court emphasized that morality and law must be kept separate. A bench comprising Justices JJ Munir and Tarun Saxena granted protection to the couple, ordering police to ensure their safety and restraining the woman's family from interference.

articles.headline: Supreme Court issues contempt notice to ASI over heritage sites
articles.summary: The Supreme Court issued a contempt notice to the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) for failing to report on the conservation status of 173 notified heritage sites in Delhi. The court summoned the ASI Director General to personally appear next month. The move follows the agency's repeated failure to submit a response regarding the protection of historic locations in the capital.

articles.headline: High Court denies Amritpal Singh parole for Parliament session
articles.summary: The Punjab and Haryana High Court dismissed a petition by jailed Khadoor Sahib MP Amritpal Singh seeking parole to attend the Budget session of Parliament. The court upheld the state government's refusal, citing that the sovereignty and security of the nation are paramount and individual liberty is subservient to maintaining public order and security of the state.

articles.headline: Women professionals challenge Karnataka's one-day menstrual leave policy
articles.summary: A group of 15 women professionals filed a petition in the Karnataka High Court seeking to quash a government notification granting one day of paid menstrual leave per month. The petitioners argue the policy is 'benevolent sexism' that reinforces gender biases and could lead to discrimination in hiring and promotions. The court has posted the matter for further hearing on April 1.

articles.headline: NCLT refuses immunity for auditors in IL&FS fraud case
articles.summary: The National Company Law Tribunal has denied immunity to audit firms Deloitte, BSR & Associates, and SRBC & Co in the IL&FS case. The ruling suggests that auditors can face residual liability if evidence indicates knowing participation in a company’s fraudulent conduct. This marks a judicial shift toward heightened scrutiny of professional advisors in financial crises.

articles.headline: Government proposes scrapping jail terms for non-compliance under Electricity Act
articles.summary: The Jan Vishwas (Amendment) Bill 2026 proposes removing imprisonment as a punishment for non-compliance with orders issued under the Electricity Act. Instead, penalties will be increased up to ₹10 lakh for serious violations. Fines for negligently wasting electricity or injuring material connected to supply will also be streamlined and raised to reach up to ₹1 lakh.

articles.headline: ED files fresh chargesheet against former RCom Director Punit Garg
articles.summary: The Enforcement Directorate filed a money laundering complaint against Punit Garg for allegedly diverting ₹40,000 crore in bank loan proceeds. Garg is accused of using diverted funds to purchase a luxury Manhattan apartment and fund personal expenses, including his children's overseas education. The agency claims the US property was offloaded during RCom’s insolvency process.

articles.headline: RBI penalises three banks and Pine Labs for regulatory lapses
articles.summary: The RBI imposed monetary penalties on Union Bank of India (₹95.4 lakh), Central Bank of India (₹63.6 lakh), and Bank of India (₹58.5 lakh) for various non-compliances. Violations included failure to report unauthorized transactions and KYC record lapses. Digital payments firm Pine Labs was also fined ₹3.1 lakh for issuing Full-KYC prepaid instruments without completing mandatory holder KYC.

articles.headline: NFRA Raises Audit Independence Concerns for Deloitte and Walker Chandiok
articles.summary: The National Financial Reporting Authority (NFRA) flagged issues with audit independence in its inspection of Deloitte Haskins & Sells and Walker Chandiok. NFRA recommended prohibiting non-audit services by non-India network firms to Indian client group entities and ensuring separation in management representation letters. Walker Chandiok was cited for non-compliance by accepting Grant Thornton network membership.

articles.headline: Sebi Bars Four Individuals in Societe Generale Frontrunning Case
articles.summary: Sebi banned Vishvanath Goswami, Umang Chaturvedi, Shyam Chaturvedi, and Vinod Kumar Chaturvedi for two years and fined them ₹5 lakh each. They allegedly frontran trades of foreign portfolio investor Societe Generale, with Atul Chaturvedi, a sales trader at Antique Stock Broking (broker for FPI), having privy to non-public information.

articles.headline: Karnataka High Court quashes FIR against Sri Sri Ravishankar
articles.summary: In a relief to Sri Sri Ravishankar, founder of the Art of Living Foundation, the Karnataka High Court quashed a suo motu First Information Report (FIR) registered against him by the Bengaluru Metropolitan Task Force. The FIR alleged encroachment on government land and a portion of Rajakaluve. The court found no iota of allegation for land grabbing.

articles.headline: Anna University professor arrested for alleged sexual harassment
articles.summary: A senior professor of Anna University’s Industrial Engineering Department, Gnana-vel Babu, was arrested on Friday following a complaint of sustained sexual harassment filed by a final-year undergraduate student. The complainant alleged inappropriate conduct over three years. The professor has been suspended by the university administration, and a case has been registered.

articles.headline: Ricky Rai's seized Lamborghini had fake number plate
articles.summary: Cubbon Park Traffic Police registered a case against Ricky Rai, son of former underworld operative Muthappa Rai, for allegedly using a fake 'fancy' registration number plate on his high-end Lamborghini Huracán. The car was seized for performing dangerous stunts. Investigations revealed the car was registered in Delhi but not re-registered in Karnataka.

articles.headline: SC highlights lapses in safeguarding sexual assault survivors' identities
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India warned of a "general indifference" among trial courts and the police towards the public disclosure of sexual assault survivors' identities, which is a criminal offense. The court intervened twice this week, directing the Supreme Court Registry to erase or redact personal identity marks from case records, affidavits, orders, and judgments.

articles.headline: EC suspends Bengal police officer after BJP-Trinamool clashes
articles.summary: The Election Commission (EC) suspended Basanti Police Station Inspector-in-Charge Avijit Paul for "serious negligence and dereliction of duty" following BJP-Trinamool Congress clashes on March 26. Several people, including police personnel, were injured in the clash, and nine people have been arrested. The EC criticized the officer for failing to make adequate police arrangements.

articles.headline: Chhattisgarh HC: Signs, gestures are substantive evidence
articles.summary: The Chhattisgarh High Court has ruled that testimony through signs and gestures shall be treated as substantive oral evidence. This decision came while dismissing the appeal of a man convicted of raping a woman with speech and hearing disabilities in 2020, rejecting the defense's contention that the victim was not a competent witness.

articles.headline: Delhi court extends NIA custody of seven foreign nationals
articles.summary: A Delhi court on Friday extended the remand of seven foreign nationals by 10 days. They are in the custody of the National Investigation Agency (NIA) over suspected links to ethnic armed groups in Myanmar. The agency had sought a 15-day custody for their interrogation.

articles.headline: Bill proposes 30-day grace period for expired driving licence
articles.summary: The Jan Vishwas (Amendment of Provisions) Bill, 2026, introduced by Minister of State for Commerce and Industry Jitin Prasada, proposes to amend the Motor Vehicle Act to allow a 30-day grace period after the expiry of a driving licence, during which it will continue to remain valid. The bill aims to decriminalize 717 provisions across 79 central acts.

articles.headline: Jan Vishwas bill proposes heavy fines and jail for land grabbing
articles.summary: The second edition of the Jan Vishwas bill, tabled in Lok Sabha, proposes that unlawful occupation of government land be met with five years of imprisonment or a fine based on the land's value. The bill aims to deter encroachment by linking penalties to the market value of the land and includes provisions for curbing public nuisance.

articles.headline: Karnataka High Court rules wedding expenses are not dowry
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court has quashed a criminal case against a family, ruling that expenses related to a wedding cannot be treated as dowry demands. The court stated that section 3 of the Dowry Prohibition Act should not be misused in matrimonial disputes without clear evidence of specific demands, emphasizing that customary wedding costs do not constitute a crime.

articles.headline: Court clears ex-MP Vijay Darda and others in coal scam
articles.summary: A Delhi court has acquitted former Rajya Sabha MP Vijay Darda, his son Devendra, and others in a coal block allocation case. The court ruled that the evidence was insufficient to prove a 'meeting of minds' or criminal conspiracy. The case, which dated back to 2012, involved allegations of misrepresenting facts to obtain coal blocks in Maharashtra.

articles.headline: Dutch court rules against Grok over non-consensual undressing images
articles.summary: An Amsterdam court has ruled that Elon Musk's xAI and its chatbot Grok must stop generating and distributing 'undressing' images of people without their consent. The court found that while AI tools have creative uses, the platform failed to implement sufficient safeguards against generating malicious deepfake content, ordering fines for future violations.

articles.headline: IPL 2026 kicks off with RCB vs Sunrisers Hyderabad clash
articles.summary: The 19th edition of the Indian Premier League begins on Saturday with Royal Challengers Bengaluru facing Sunrisers Hyderabad at the Chinnaswamy Stadium. Ahead of the opener, the Karnataka State Cricket Association agreed to provide two tickets to each state MLA following their demand for VIP access. The season starts amid high valuations for franchises, with RCB valued at $1.78 billion.

articles.headline: Backlash against IOC genetic testing policy for women athletes
articles.summary: Human rights groups and women athletes have criticized the International Olympic Committee's new gender policy mandating genetic testing for the 2028 Summer Olympics. A group of 69 experts issued a statement calling the guidelines 'stigma-based' and a violation of privacy. Nine African track stars, including Caster Semenya, wrote to the IOC urging a more inclusive definition of womanhood.

articles.headline: AIFF receives ₹2,130 crore commercial bid for domestic competitions
articles.summary: The All India Football Federation (AIFF) received a 20-year commercial bid worth ₹2,130 crore from Genius Sports for ISL and cup competition rights. The deal, worth nearly ₹64 crore annually, would cover men's domestic events. A separate ₹5 crore bid for the Indian Women's League was also received. The AIFF executive committee will discuss the options this weekend.

articles.headline: KSCA Agrees to Provide IPL Tickets for MLAs
articles.summary: The Karnataka State Cricketers Association (KSCA) agreed to provide VIP tickets to MLAs, MLCs, and MPs for upcoming IPL matches in Bengaluru. MLAs had previously raised demands in the assembly for tickets. The KSCA will arrange two VIP tickets for Saturday's match and three for the April 5 match.

articles.headline: IPL 2026 Season Begins Amid Global Turmoil
articles.summary: The 2026 Indian Premier League (IPL) season kicks off, with the first match at Chinnaswamy Stadium. The tournament begins without an opening ceremony as a quiet tribute to last year's tragic events. Despite global market turmoil, IPL franchises continue to attract astronomical valuations, highlighting its enduring popularity and economic significance.

articles.headline: Tonali Scores Decisive Goal, Keeps Italy's World Cup Hopes Alive
articles.summary: Sandro Tonali scored the decisive goal, a half-volley, and assisted another, leading Italy to a 2-0 victory over Northern Ireland in the European playoff semifinals. Playing in his idol Gattuso's former central midfield position, Tonali's 'most important goal of my career' keeps Italy's World Cup hopes alive.

articles.headline: Two-time Olympic Medallist Gurjant Singh Retires from Hockey
articles.summary: India's two-time Olympic medallist Gurjant Singh, 31, has retired from international hockey due to a back injury and losing his place in the national team. The forward represented India in 130 matches, helping the team win back-to-back bronze medals at the Tokyo and Paris Olympics.

articles.headline: Iran Bans Sports Teams from Visiting 'Hostile' Countries
articles.summary: Iran has banned its sports teams from traveling to countries it considers 'hostile,' as reported by state TV. The Ministry of Sports specifically singled out Tractor FC's scheduled football game in Saudi Arabia next month, without mentioning the upcoming World Cup in North America.

articles.headline: France Calls IOC Gender Testing Rule for Women's Events 'Step Backwards'
articles.summary: France's sports minister criticised the International Olympic Committee's (IOC) decision to introduce genetic testing for women's events as a 'step backwards.' She warned it raises major ethical, legal, and scientific concerns. The rule states only biological females are eligible, effectively barring transgender athletes.

articles.headline: Karnataka Chief Secretary reviews IPL security at Chinnaswamy Stadium
articles.summary: Karnataka Chief Secretary Shalini Rajneesh chaired a meeting with senior police officials and Karnataka State Cricket Association office-bearers to review safety measures for the IPL match at M. Chinnaswamy Stadium. Discussions included security inside and around the stadium, traffic management, parking, drinking water availability, and strengthening security at metro stations following last year's stampede.

articles.headline: IPL opener: Defending champion RCB takes on SRH
articles.summary: The 19th edition of the Indian Premier League kicks off with defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) taking on Sunrisers Hyderabad (SRH) in the opening encounter at the M. Chinnaswamy Stadium in Bengaluru on Saturday. This highly anticipated match sets the stage for a new season of cricketing action.

articles.headline: KSCA promises free IPL passes to Karnataka legislators
articles.summary: Following complaints from legislators, KSCA president Venkatesh Prasad met Chief Minister Siddaramaiah and Deputy Chief Minister D.K. Shivakumar. He assured them that two free passes would be given to each legislator for Saturday's opening match between RCB and SRH, and four free passes for subsequent IPL matches in Bengaluru.

articles.headline: Anupama Ramachandran honored for India's snooker ascent
articles.summary: Anupama Ramachandran, a snooker player from Chennai, received the Excellence in Sports Award. She won the gold medal at the IBSF World Open Under-16 Championship in Russia in 2017 and became the first Indian woman to win the IBSF Women’s World Snooker Championship in November 2025. She also holds the Indian record for the highest break by a woman player (119).

articles.headline: Kasthuri Rajamani honored as top name in Indian para powerlifting
articles.summary: Para powerlifter Kasthuri Rajamani received The Hindu Achievement in Surpassing Disability Award. In 2023, she won two gold medals at the IWAS World Games and became the first Indian woman in her category to lift over 100 kg at the Asian Games. She represented India at the Paris Paralympics in 2024 and secured a bronze medal at the World Cup the same year.

articles.headline: IPL dynamics shift to Indian core amid foreign recruit uncertainty
articles.summary: IPL dynamics are rebalancing, with franchises increasingly relying on abundant domestic talent and home-grown captains. Foreign recruits are now used more to plug supply gaps rather than defining teams. Untimely injuries to overseas stars like Pat Cummins (SRH) and Josh Hazlewood (RCB) further reinforce this shift, focusing the spotlight firmly on the Indian core.

articles.headline: RCB coach Andy Flower says Virat Kohli is "fit and hungry"
articles.summary: Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) head coach Andy Flower praised Virat Kohli, stating he looks "on top of his game," "very fit, very lean, very hungry," and "at the peak of his prowess" as RCB prepares for the IPL 2026 opener. Flower drew a comparison between Kohli's commitment and Sachin Tendulkar's practice ethic.

articles.headline: RCB, PBKS, MI, DC predicted to make IPL top-four
articles.summary: A prediction for the IPL 2026 top-four teams includes Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB), Punjab Kings (PBKS), Mumbai Indians (MI), and Delhi Capitals (DC). Factors like past history, player strengths, team compositions, and RCB's home advantage at M. Chinnaswamy Stadium were considered. Mumbai Indians, with a strong domestic nucleus, are chasing a crown last won in 2020.

articles.headline: Hazlewood unavailable for RCB's IPL season opener
articles.summary: Royal Challengers Bengaluru coach Andy Flower revealed that Australian pace stalwart Josh Hazlewood will not feature in Saturday’s IPL season opener against Sunrisers Hyderabad. Flower stated Hazlewood has just landed, and his fitness will be closely monitored ahead of his entrance into the IPL.

articles.headline: SRH's attacking style challenging on tricky surfaces: Vettori
articles.summary: Sunrisers Hyderabad (SRH) head coach Daniel Vettori admitted that the team’s all-out attacking approach will be a challenge on tricky surfaces. He stated that while SRH batters are difficult to stop on really good surfaces, adapting to more challenging conditions will be the key to success in the upcoming IPL season.

articles.headline: RCB pacers Mangesh, Abhinandan, Rasikh compete for spot
articles.summary: Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) head coach Andy Flower stated that uncapped Indian pacers Mangesh Yadav, Abhinandan Singh, and Rasikh Salam are competing for a place in the team. This comes as key pacers like Yash Dayal and Josh Hazlewood are currently out of action for the defending champions.

articles.headline: Batting No. 4 ideal for Klaasen to settle, explode: Vettori
articles.summary: Sunrisers Hyderabad (SRH) head coach Daniel Vettori believes that Heinrich Klaasen batting at No. 4 is an ideal spot for him. Vettori explained that this position gives Klaasen some time to settle in, take a couple of balls, and then be the explosive batter that he is for the team.

articles.headline: Sai Sudharsan credits Shubman Gill as biggest batting influence
articles.summary: Gujarat Titans batter Sai Sudharsan termed captain Shubman Gill as the biggest influence on his batting, learning from playing alongside him and watching him in the nets. Sudharsan emphasized the instinctive and adaptable nature of their opening partnership, where both focus on reacting well rather than having pre-determined notions, and the non-judgemental culture at GT.

articles.headline: Hardik Singh, Navneet Kaur win Hockey India Player-of-the-Year
articles.summary: Hardik Singh and Navneet Kaur walked away with the Player-of-the-Year awards in the men’s and women’s categories, respectively, at the eighth annual Hockey India awards held on Friday. Hardik, receiving the honor for the third consecutive time, and Navneet earned a prize money of ₹20 lakh each. Zafar Iqbal received the Major Dhyan Chand Lifetime Achievement Award.

articles.headline: Klopp hails Salah as Liverpool's 'all-time great'
articles.summary: Former Liverpool manager Jurgen Klopp believes Mohamed Salah will leave the club as one of the "all-time greats," citing his "unmatched numbers" that are likely to prove tough for future generations at Anfield. Klopp stated Salah was part of the best front three in world football for a long time.

articles.headline: Indian archers secure rich haul at Asia Cup Stage-1
articles.summary: Indian archers collected eight more medals, including two gold in compound men’s individual and mixed team events, on the concluding day of the Asia Cup Stage-1 archery tournament in Bangkok. Uday Kamboj defeated compatriot Prathamesh Jawkar 145-144 in the compound men's individual final, with Rajat Chauhan bagging bronze for a clean sweep.

articles.headline: Verstappen tells British journalist to leave press conference
articles.summary: Max Verstappen sparked a verbal confrontation ahead of the Japanese Grand Prix on Thursday, refusing to start his press conference until a British journalist left the room. Verstappen stated, "I’m not speaking before he’s leaving," gesturing at Giles Richards, a journalist from The Guardian newspaper, who subsequently left.

articles.headline: Mysore District-A boys, Viveks SC girls win basketball matches
articles.summary: Mysore District-A boys beat MCHS BC 83-66 in a boys’ match at the State junior basketball championship in Bengaluru on Friday. In a girls’ category game, Viveks SC defeated Appaiah BC 44-34, securing wins in their respective matches.

articles.headline: Sarvagnanagar defeats Yeshwanthpura 13-0 in football
articles.summary: Sarvagnanagar trounced Yeshwanthpura 13-0 in the KSFA-Vaishnavi Chief Minister Cup football tournament in Bengaluru on Friday. In the day’s other match, Hebbal beat Rajaji Nagar 5-2, showcasing strong performances in the local competition.

articles.headline: Timeless Vision to redeem herself in Donegal Plate race
articles.summary: The five-year-old mare Timeless Vision, who ran second in her last start, is expected to redeem herself in the Donegal Plate, the main event of the races to be held in Hyderabad on Saturday. She is predicted to put in a strong performance.

articles.headline: Italy, Sweden win World Cup playoff semifinals
articles.summary: Italy’s bid to end a 12-year World Cup absence will continue after beating Northern Ireland 2-0 to reach the playoff final, where they face Bosnia and Herzegovina. Viktor Gyokeres scored a hat-trick as Sweden brushed Ukraine aside 3-1, setting up a showdown with Poland. The Czech Republic also advanced on penalties.

articles.headline: Chacarra, Jarvis lead on rain-marred Hero Indian Open
articles.summary: Eugenio Chacarra and Daniel Jarvis took charge on a rain-marred day two of the Hero Indian Open. Play was delayed for almost an hour and a half due to rain and bad light, pushing tee-offs. Jarvis posted a 64 for the best performance over the first two days, high on confidence after recent title wins.

articles.headline: Auqib Nabi aims to stay true to strengths in maiden IPL
articles.summary: Jammu & Kashmir pacer Auqib Nabi, picked up by Delhi Capitals for ₹8.4 crore, aims to stick to his strengths of swinging the new ball and making early breakthroughs in his maiden IPL season. With 60 Ranji Trophy scalps last season, he is also working on mastering yorkers and slower balls for the T20 format.

articles.headline: Kochi gears up for international football after decade-long gap
articles.summary: Kochi is gearing up for its date with international football after a decade-long gap, as India takes on Hong Kong in an Asian Cup qualifier at the Jawaharlal Nehru Stadium on Tuesday. The visiting team arrived in Kochi on Friday, and preparations are in full swing, with hopes for a strong crowd despite prior issues with stadium use.

articles.headline: Kerala Blasters appoint Ashley Westwood as head coach
articles.summary: Kerala Blasters has appointed Ashley Westwood as the head coach till the end of the ongoing ISL season, with immediate effect. Westwood previously had a successful stint with Bengaluru FC, guiding them to two I-League titles and a Federation Cup triumph. The club confirmed it parted ways with former coach David Catala via mutual agreement.

articles.headline: Stern test awaits Praggnanandhaa at FIDE Candidates tournament
articles.summary: R. Praggnanandhaa faces a stern test in the FIDE Candidates tournament 2026, beginning in Papho, Cyprus, on Saturday. He is the only Indian in the Open event, a mix of experienced and young elite chess players including Hikaru Nakamura and Fabiano Caruana. Praggnanandhaa will aim to go all the way and earn a shot at his compatriot D. Gukesh.

articles.headline: Manpreet Singh dropped from Indian hockey camp ahead of Olympics
articles.summary: Veteran midfielder and former captain Manpreet Singh was a notable exclusion from the Indian men's hockey national camp. Hockey India president Dilip Tirkey stated the veteran was dropped to allow the management to focus on younger players as they prepare for the 2028 Olympics. Manpreet retires with a record 412 international appearances.

articles.headline: IPL 2026 kicks off as RCB defends title against SRH
articles.summary: The 2026 IPL season begins tonight at the Chinnaswamy Stadium with defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru taking on Sunrisers Hyderabad. RCB, led by Ishan Kishan, will look to maintain their home stronghold, while SRH, coached by Daniel Vettori, faces injury concerns with pacer Pat Cummins. The match starts at 7:30 PM.

articles.headline: Forest minister orders inquiry into tiger death in snare
articles.summary: Karnataka Forest Minister Eshwar Khandre ordered an inquiry into the death of a three-year-old tiger caught in an illegal snare near Nagarahole National Park. The minister directed the Chief Conservator of Forests to lead a probe into potential patrolling lapses. Legal action will be taken against those responsible for setting the trap on private land near the forest boundary.

articles.headline: India's key reservoirs half empty as rain deficiency persists
articles.summary: Half of India's 166 major reservoirs are currently at less than 50% capacity, raising concerns for the upcoming kharif season. The Central Water Commission reported that widespread rain deficiency and a high probability of El Nino after June pose significant risks to agriculture. Storage in the southern region is particularly low at 38.2% of total capacity.

articles.headline: Musi Riverfront project triggers eviction fears among residents
articles.summary: The Telangana government's ambitious Musi Riverfront Development Project in Hyderabad faces strong opposition from residents over potential displacement and uncertainty regarding compensation. Environmentalists also criticize the project's lack of focus on addressing pollution at its source and proper ecological restoration of the river, which carries sewage and industrial effluents.

articles.headline: Forest Minister seeks report on State vets visiting Anant Ambani's Vantara
articles.summary: Karnataka Forest Minister Eshwar B. Khandre has sought a detailed report from the State Forest Department regarding alleged visits by senior officials and veterinarians to Vantara, Anant Ambani's 3,500-acre private zoo in Gujarat. The request follows a trainee veterinarian's recent death after a hippopotamus attack in Shivamogga, where staff from Vantara had advised thermal imaging.

articles.headline: CBSE announces hybrid assessment for Gulf Class 12 students
articles.summary: The CBSE will use a hybrid assessment method for nearly 23,000 Class 12 students in the Middle East due to the prevailing conflict. The board cancelled examinations from March 1 onwards and will declare results using a formula that combines school-based assessments with performance in exams conducted in February. The move affects schools across seven West Asian countries.

articles.headline: Karnataka scraps marks for SSLC third language, implements grading
articles.summary: The Karnataka government has decided not to award marks for the third language, including Hindi, in the SSLC final results to reduce academic pressure. Instead, a grading system will be followed, applicable to the ongoing exam. Minister Madhu Bangarappa stated that 1.64 lakh students failed in the third language last year, and 1.48 lakh failed in Hindi.

articles.headline: Geeta Dharmarajan honored for turning stories into agents of change
articles.summary: Geeta Dharmarajan received The Hindu Excellence in Academic and Education Award. In 1988, she founded Katha, rooted in the belief that access to stories can transform lives. The Katha Lab School now educates over 2,000 students, and her Story Pedagogy model is used across government schools, with Katha’s 400-plus books reaching over a million children.

articles.headline: Grades to replace marks in Karnataka SSLC third language exam
articles.summary: The Karnataka government has decided to replace marks with grades (A, B, C, or D) for the third language in SSLC exams. This change, affecting subjects like Hindi and Kannada, aims to reduce student stress. Students must still take the exam, but total SSLC marks will be reduced from 625 to 525 as third language scores will no longer affect the overall result.

articles.headline: New TV Ratings Policy 2026 to include OTT platforms
articles.summary: The government notified the TV Ratings Policy 2026, expanding the scope of viewership data to cover OTT platforms and connected TVs. The policy increases the required sample size to 80,000 homes and mandates that at least 50% of the board of rating agencies must be independent directors. It also introduces regular audits to prevent data manipulation in the broadcasting sector.

articles.headline: WhatsApp rolls out dual accounts and AI features for iPhone
articles.summary: WhatsApp announced an update allowing iPhone users to run two accounts on a single device simultaneously. The platform also introduced 'Meta AI' tools for photo editing and drafting messages within chats. Other updates include simplified chat transfers from iOS to Android and predictive sticker suggestions based on user typing context, following a recent rollout for Android users.

articles.headline: RBI Proposes Kill Switch for All Digital Payments to Combat Fraud
articles.summary: The RBI's Payments Vision Document 2028 proposes extending a 'on/off' facility, currently for card transactions, to all digital payment modes. This will enable users to manage transaction capabilities and strengthen confidence in the ecosystem, enhancing consumer protection and improving system resilience against fraud.

articles.headline: Firms Fear Strict DPDP Act Verification, High Compliance Costs
articles.summary: A survey by Esya Centre reveals 85% of businesses fear they cannot meet strict data verification norms and rising compliance costs under the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act. 30% expect compliance costs to exceed 10% of turnover, and most do not fully understand the Act's implications for their operations.

articles.headline: Facebook Takes Down Kerala Oppn Leader's Interview After Police Notice
articles.summary: A segment of an interview with Kerala Leader of Opposition VD Satheesan (Congress), aired on a TV channel, was removed by Facebook following a notice from the Kerala police cyber wing. The police sought to block access due to 'vulgar remarks' under the post, leading to the video itself being removed.

articles.headline: Omax Autos Confirms Ransomware Attack on IT Infrastructure
articles.summary: Auto components maker Omax Autos confirmed a ransomware attack on its IT infrastructure, discovered on March 26. The company is assessing potential loss or impact, but stated core systems and operations are unaffected. A detailed investigation and remedial actions are underway.

articles.headline: Indian IT Firms Increase Acquisitions, Eye Cloud, AI, and Data Verticals
articles.summary: Indian IT services companies are projected to spend $6.5-7 billion on acquisitions in 2026 (up from $5 billion in 2025), targeting 30-35 deals. Focus areas include Cloud, data verticals, enterprise platforms, and AI. This inorganic growth strategy aims to boost revenue amid a challenging economic environment, with major players like Infosys and HCLTech making significant recent acquisitions.

articles.headline: RBI to Review Cross-Border Payments Framework, Explore Small Payment Providers
articles.summary: The RBI, in its Payments Vision Document 2028, will comprehensively review the cross-border payments ecosystem to reduce frictions and align with global best practices. It will explore interoperability in TReDS, strengthen digital payment research, and consider a new class of small payment system providers not requiring initial authorization.

articles.headline: Anthropic Considering October IPO Amid AI Race with OpenAI
articles.summary: AI company Anthropic, maker of the Claude chatbot, is reportedly considering an IPO as early as October, having held preliminary talks with Wall Street banks. A listing could value the company over $60 billion, intensifying its competition with rival OpenAI in the rapidly evolving artificial intelligence sector.

articles.headline: AI Music's Rise and Impact on Human Creativity Debated
articles.summary: AI-generated music, exemplified by Xania Monet topping charts, is challenging human creativity. Research shows AI music can be emotionally stirring. Artists like Billie Eilish protest AI models trained on their work, fearing it will devalue human art. Despite growing industry adoption, concerns persist over AI's long-term impact on artists and the marketplace.

articles.headline: Xiaomi Launches Redmi 15A 5G Smartphone in India
articles.summary: Xiaomi introduced the Redmi 15A 5G smartphone in India, featuring a 6.9-inch HD+ display, UNISOC T8300 chip, and 6,300mAh battery. It supports AI features like Google Gemini and Circle to Search. The device will be available from April 3, with prices starting at ₹12,999.

articles.headline: Apple Discontinues Mac Pro, Shifts Focus to Mac Studio
articles.summary: Apple has removed the Mac Pro from its online store, confirming its discontinuation without a planned successor. The company will now focus on the Mac Studio as its primary desktop offering for power users. This move streamlines Apple’s professional desktop lineup.

articles.headline: Iran-linked hackers claim breach of FBI director's email
articles.summary: The Iran-linked hacker group Handala Hack Team publicly claimed the breach of FBI Director Kash Patel’s personal inbox, publishing photographs of the director and his purported resume to the Internet. On their website, the group stated that Mr. Patel "will now find his name among the list of successfully hacked victims."

articles.headline: Drug Regulator Steps Up Oversight of GLP-1 Drug Distribution Chain
articles.summary: India's drug regulator instructed state drug controllers to strengthen monitoring of the GLP-1-based weight loss drug supply chain. This is to ensure strict adherence to approval conditions, prevent diversion into unauthorized channels, and crack down on non-compliant advertisements across all media platforms.

articles.headline: Hetero launches generic semaglutide portfolio in overseas markets
articles.summary: Hyderabad-based Hetero has launched generic semaglutide in over 75 countries, targeting type 2 diabetes and obesity. While awaiting regulatory approval in India, initial launches are underway in Africa, Asia, and West Asia. The portfolio features multi-dose disposable pen devices marketed under brands like Truglyx and Rolmodl to improve global access to cardio-metabolic therapies.

articles.headline: Novartis to acquire Excellergy Inc in $2 billion biotech deal
articles.summary: Swiss pharmaceutical giant Novartis AG has agreed to buy Excellergy Inc for as much as $2 billion. The deal focuses on strengthening Novartis' portfolio of treatments for food allergies and immunology. The acquisition comes as the company seeks new revenue sources to offset generic competition for its older blockbuster drugs.

articles.headline: Lokayukta flags ICU lapses, private ambulance nexus at Victoria Hospital
articles.summary: The Karnataka Lokayukta flagged six unused ICU beds due to non-functional ventilators, the presence of expired medicines, and an alleged nexus between private ambulance operators and middlemen diverting patients at Victoria Hospital. The authority has asked the State government to consider regulating private ambulance services through a prepaid system and has sought a compliance report by April 23.

articles.headline: Swathy Rohit honored for inclusive school-based healthcare platform
articles.summary: Swathy Rohit, founder of Health Basix, received the Excellence in Entrepreneurship Award. She built a school-based healthcare platform that delivers physical, mental, and special education services to children, focusing on early intervention and improving access to care across schools. She is also a co-founder of the Centre for Mental Health Policy.

articles.headline: Revathi Raj honored for redefining care for children with cancer
articles.summary: Revathi Raj, Senior Consultant in Paediatric Haematology Oncology, received The Hindu Excellence in Health and Hygiene Award. Joining Apollo Hospitals, Chennai, in 2002, she has performed over 1,500 paediatric bone marrow transplants, including India’s first successful unrelated donor transplant. She also helped over 500 children recover from thalassaemia.

articles.headline: IIT Kharagpur applies to launch post-graduate MD medical courses
articles.summary: IIT Kharagpur has submitted an application to the National Medical Council to introduce MD programs in six specialisations, including pathology and microbiology. If approved, it will become the first IIT in India to offer a post-graduate medical degree, with 20 seats planned for the inaugural program at the B.C. Roy Multi-Speciality Medical Research Centre.

articles.headline: Book Explores Lives of Bollywood's Child Actors After Fame
articles.summary: "Behind the Big Screen: The Untold Stories of Bollywood’s Child Actors" by Sunanda Mehta and Suchitra Iyer examines the lives of child actors from the 1960s-90s. Through interviews, it reveals contrasting experiences—fond memories vs. scarring exploitation—and highlights the crucial roles of parental guidance, education, and legal protections.

articles.headline: Oscars to Move to Downtown Los Angeles' Peacock Theater in 2029
articles.summary: The Academy Awards will move from Hollywood’s Dolby Theater to the Peacock Theater at L.A. Live in downtown Los Angeles starting in 2029, coinciding with its shift to YouTube. The deal with AEG runs through 2039, providing a larger venue. This marks a return to downtown for the Oscars, which was held there intermittently until 2002.

articles.headline: Sudha Ragunathan receives Lifetime Achievement Award for Carnatic music
articles.summary: Carnatic vocalist Sudha Ragunathan bagged The Hindu Lifetime Achievement Award for her close to five decades of excellence. Recognized with the Padma Bhushan in 2015 and the Sangeet Natak Akademi Award in 2022, she is the only Carnatic musician after M.S. Subbulakshmi to have performed at the United Nations.

articles.headline: Kapila Venu honored for advancing ancient Kutiyattam theatre
articles.summary: Kapila Venu, a proponent of Kutiyattam, one of the world’s oldest-living theatre traditions, received The Hindu Excellence in Art, Music and Culture Award. A disciple of Guru Ammannur Madhava Chakyar, she has spent over two decades performing, teaching, and conducting workshops worldwide, embodying rare commitment to this ancient form.

articles.headline: Radikaa Sarathkumar honored for vigour and versatility in acting
articles.summary: Actor and producer Radikaa Sarathkumar received the Excellence in Entertainment Award for her career spanning over four decades across Tamil, Telugu, Malayalam, Kannada, and Hindi cinema, appearing in over 300 films. Introduced by director Bharathiraja, she established herself as a versatile performer and won the National Award for Best Producer in 1984.

articles.headline: Sreeja Ravi, Raveena Ravi receive Generational Pride Award
articles.summary: Mother-daughter duo Sreeja Ravi and Raveena Ravi were honored with The Hindu GRT Generational Pride Award for their contributions to cinema. Sreeja has lent her voice to over 2,000 films and received five Kerala State Film Awards. Raveena is also a sought-after voice artist and actor, having headlined twelve films.

articles.headline: Metformin drug for diabetes found to have positive brain effects
articles.summary: A study published in Science Advances reveals that the widely used diabetes drug metformin can cross the blood-brain barrier and help treat type 2 diabetes by acting on the brain’s control system. Research shows the drug activates the protein Rapt, which protects nerve cells and helps regulate blood sugar, suggesting new therapeutic pathways for metabolic disorders.

articles.headline: US Weighs Sending 10,000 Extra Troops to Middle East Amid Iran Tensions
articles.summary: The US is weighing sending up to 10,000 extra troops to the Middle/West Asia, as reported by The Wall Street Journal. This potential deployment, joining thousands already ordered, comes amid escalating tensions with Iran and speculation of a ground operation. President Trump stated all military options are available but aims to avoid a large-scale ground invasion, despite insisting Tehran is taking part in peace talks.

articles.headline: Balendra Shah Sworn in as Nepal's Youngest PM After Landslide Win
articles.summary: Rapper-turned-politician Balendra Shah, 35, was sworn in as Nepal's 47th and youngest Prime Minister after his Rastriya Swatantra Party's landslide victory. He pledged loyalty to the constitution and named a small cabinet, including economist Swarnim Wagle (finance). PM Modi congratulated him, hoping for strengthened India-Nepal relations and regional security cooperation.

articles.headline: Trump Extends Iran Energy Strike Pause Amid Hormuz Standoff
articles.summary: US President Trump extended the deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz until April 6/7, or face strikes on energy plants. This came after Iran rejected his proposal and denied talks, despite Trump's claims of successful negotiations. Iran's IRGC reiterated shipping ban through Hormuz for "enemies' allies," urging civilians near US bases to evacuate. The conflict has blocked 20% of global oil supplies.

articles.headline: Rapper-Engineer Balendra Shah Sworn in as Nepal's Youngest PM
articles.summary: Rapper-engineer Balendra 'Balen' Shah, 35, was sworn in as Nepal's 47th and youngest Prime Minister by President Ram Chandra Paudel. The Rastriya Swatantra Party leader, also the first from the Madhes region, restructured his cabinet. He faces immediate challenges managing public expectations and tackling economic issues exacerbated by the West Asia war, including rising fuel prices and cooking gas rationing.

articles.headline: Ukraine, Saudi Arabia Sign Defence Cooperation Agreement
articles.summary: Ukraine President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced a significant defence cooperation agreement with Saudi Arabia during his visit. The pact lays the foundation for future contracts, technological collaboration, and investment. This comes as Ukraine faces uncertainty over US military supplies due to the West Asia conflict. Ukrainian drone experts are also assisting five Gulf countries against Iran's drone attacks.

articles.headline: Govt Cuts Fuel Excise Duty by ₹10/L to Aid OMCs, Maintain Prices
articles.summary: The Centre cut special additional excise duty on petrol and diesel by ₹10/litre to offset losses for OMCs and protect consumers. Retail pump prices remain unchanged. New export duties were reimposed on diesel (₹21.5/L) and ATF (₹29.5/L) to ensure domestic availability. This temporary measure, costing the exchequer ₹1.70 lakh crore annually, aims to stabilize the market amid global oil price hikes and West Asia war.

articles.headline: Indian Rupee Hits Record Low 94.81/USD Amid West Asia Conflict
articles.summary: The Indian rupee fell to a record 94.81 against the dollar on Friday, breaching 94 for the first time. This 4% decline in the session, and 11% over the fiscal year, marks its worst performance in over a decade. The fall is attributed to surging crude oil prices, heavy foreign investor sell-offs, and West Asia conflict, which also saw Indian equities tumble 2%. Analysts forecast 97/$.

articles.headline: India's Forex Reserves Drop Below $700 Billion Amid Gold Price Fall
articles.summary: India's forex reserves fell by $11.41 billion to $698.35 billion for the week ended March 20, dropping below $700 billion for the first time in two months. This marks a $30.14 billion decline in three weeks, attributed mainly to a $13.49 billion fall in gold reserves, despite a rise in foreign currency assets. Economists warn of further pressure due to the West Asia crisis and RBI's dollar sales.

articles.headline: SEBI Extends Agri Derivatives Trading Ban Till March 2027
articles.summary: SEBI extended the suspension of derivatives trading in seven key agricultural commodities, including wheat, paddy, chana, mustard seeds, and moong, until March 31, 2027. This measure, originally issued in 2021, aims to curb price volatility, excessive speculation, and control food inflation by prohibiting fresh futures trading in these essential food items for another year.

articles.headline: RBI Caps Banks' Rupee Net Open Positions at $100 Million
articles.summary: The RBI capped banks' net open positions in the rupee at $100 million at the end of each business day, effective April 10. This move, the first direct curb in nearly 15 years, aims to tighten oversight in the forex market, curb speculative positions, and reduce pressure on the rupee, which depreciated 3.5% this month.

articles.headline: Centre Increases Commercial LPG Allocation for Industries by 20%
articles.summary: The MoPNG increased commercial LPG allocation for industries by 20%, bringing the total to 70% of pre-crisis levels. This offers respite to commercial entities, prioritizing steel, auto, textile, dye, chemicals, and plastics sectors. The move supports labour-intensive and essential industries, especially those needing LPG for specialized heating that cannot be substituted by natural gas.

articles.headline: Centre Cuts Fuel Excise Duty by ₹10/Litre; Prices Unchanged
articles.summary: The Centre cut special additional excise duty on petrol and diesel by ₹10/litre to reduce under-recoveries for state-run OMCs; consumer fuel prices remain unchanged. A windfall gain tax on export-bound diesel and ATF was reintroduced/hiked. This move is expected to cost Central revenues ₹5,500 crore over 15 days, with fortnightly reviews to continue.

articles.headline: Macquarie Predicts $200 Oil if Iran War Closes Hormuz Until June
articles.summary: Macquarie Group analysts predict Brent crude could hit $200 a barrel with 40% probability if the US-Israel-Iran war continues through June, keeping the Strait of Hormuz closed. This would lead to historically high real prices and massive global oil demand destruction. Despite a brief respite from Trump's strike pause extension, the waterway closure severely restricts energy flows. Brent traded near $107-110, touching $119 this month.

articles.headline: UP Cancels ₹25,000 Cr MoU with Bengaluru AI Startup Puch AI
articles.summary: The Uttar Pradesh government scrapped its ₹25,000 crore MoU with Bengaluru AI startup Puch AI, citing insufficient net worth and financial credibility. The less-than-a-year-old firm, which proposed developing AI parks and a data center/university, failed to furnish required financial details during InvestUP's due diligence, leading to immediate cancellation in the interest of probity.

articles.headline: Pernod Ricard, Brown-Forman Discuss Merger Amid Industry Downturn
articles.summary: Spirits giants Pernod Ricard ($18B market value) and Brown-Forman ($11.8B market value, owner of Jack Daniel's) are discussing a merger. This stock-based deal, allowing founding families to retain stakes, could create a powerhouse with brands like Absolut Vodka. The consolidation comes amidst an industry downturn, with Pernod seeking to expand market value.

articles.headline: RBI Fines Union, Central, BoI, Pine Labs for Regulatory Violations
articles.summary: The RBI fined Union Bank of India (₹95.40 lakh), Central Bank of India (₹63.60 lakh), Bank of India (₹58.50 lakh), and Pine Labs (₹3.10 lakh) for various regulatory violations. These included issues like failure to credit unauthorised electronic transactions, incomplete KYC for prepaid instruments, ad hoc charges, and non-compliance with Priority Sector Lending norms.

articles.headline: Fino Payments Bank Halts MD Rishi Gupta's Reappointment After Arrest
articles.summary: Fino Payments Bank withdrew its proposal to reappoint MD & CEO Rishi Gupta, pending an RBI review of his 'fit and proper' status and citing corporate governance. The decision follows Gupta's February 27 arrest in a GST evasion case, for which he was granted bail. His candidature remains unaffected and will be reconsidered, subject to regulatory requirements and RBI approval.

articles.headline: Coal India to Invest ₹3,300 Cr in 8 Coking Coal Washeries by FY30
articles.summary: Coal India Limited (CIL) will invest ₹3,300 crore to establish eight new coking coal washeries, operational by FY30. These units, including in Central Coalfields and Bharat Coking Coal, will have a combined washing capacity of 21.5 million tonnes per year. The initiative aims to improve domestic coal quality and reduce India's import dependence for the steel sector.

articles.headline: Trump's Signature to Appear on New US Dollar Bills
articles.summary: The U.S. Treasury plans to feature President Donald Trump’s signature on all new U.S. paper currency, alongside Secretary Scott Bessent. This historic change, coinciding with America's 250th anniversary, marks the first time a sitting President's signature will appear on currency since 1861, traditionally bearing only the Treasury Secretary and Treasurer's signatures.

articles.headline: Adani Ports Establishes India's First Ports of Refuge for Maritime Safety
articles.summary: Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone Ltd (APSEZ) operationalized India’s first Ports of Refuge (PoR) at Dighi (west coast) and Gopalpur (east coast). This fills a long-standing gap in maritime emergency infrastructure, providing designated locations for ships to seek shelter, stabilize conditions, limit environmental damage, and access salvage, firefighting, and pollution control services.

articles.headline: Eight Killed, 20 Injured as Pickup Rams Truck in UP's Kaushambi
articles.summary: Eight people, including five women and three children, were killed and 20 injured on Friday when a pickup truck carrying 12 devotees rammed a stationary trailer on the National Highway in UP's Kaushambi district. The victims were returning from the 'Maa Sheetla' shrine. Police are investigating, and CM Yogi Adityanath directed expedited relief for the accident victims.

articles.headline: Avalanche Kills 7, Injures 5 on Zojila Pass; Highway Closed
articles.summary: A snow avalanche hit the zero point area of the high-altitude Zojila Pass on the Srinagar-Leh National Highway on Friday, killing seven (five confirmed, two missing) and injuring five. The pass, connecting Kashmir Valley with Ladakh, was open for one-way traffic when the incident occurred. The highway is now closed, with rescue operations ongoing and monitored by Ladakh LG and Union Minister.

articles.headline: DAC Clears Record ₹2.38 Lakh Cr Defence Acquisitions for FY26
articles.summary: The Defence Acquisition Council, chaired by Rajnath Singh, cleared ₹2.38-2.4 lakh crore capital acquisition proposals in its final FY26 meeting. This brings total fiscal year approvals to a record ₹6.73 lakh crore. Acquisitions include 60 medium transport aircraft, additional Russian S-400 missile systems, air defence tracked systems, and domestic 155mm Dhanush gun systems, enhancing India's combat, surveillance, and mobility capabilities.

articles.headline: India Signs ₹858 Cr Defence Deals with Russia, US Firms
articles.summary: The Ministry of Defence signed ₹858 crore defence deals, reinforcing India's balanced partnerships. This includes a ₹445 crore contract with Russia's Rosoboronexport for Tunguska Air Defence Missile Systems for the Army—a self-propelled system striking low-flying targets within 8km. Additionally, a ₹413 crore deal was signed with Boeing India for Navy's P-8I maritime reconnaissance fleet maintenance.

articles.headline: Jan Vishwas Bill 2.0 Tabled to Decriminalise 717 Minor Offences
articles.summary: The government introduced the Jan Vishwas (Amendment of Provisions) Bill, 2026, in Lok Sabha, aiming to decriminalise 717 provisions across 79 central acts. Incorporating select committee recommendations, it seeks to promote ease of doing business and living by replacing jail terms for minor offences (e.g., Electricity, Cattle Trespass Acts) with graded monetary penalties. Some Congress members opposed it, citing basic structure infringement.
